rules.rule: Decorative avatar/social-proof signals (avatar row, +count chip below hero CTA) must NOT be sized as 44px tap targets since they are non-interactive visual signals; interactive elements should still meet platform touch minimums
rules.metric: min(boundingClientRect.width, height) of decorative .flex avatars / non-anchor circular badges
rules.threshold: decorative avatars stay below 44px AND carry no click handler/href; interactive controls remain tappable
rules.detect: JS: select hero avatar nodes (e.g. div.w-7.h-7.rounded-full not wrapped in <a>/<button>); assert they have no onclick/href and Math.min(w,h) < 44; flag any decorative signal mistakenly given an interactive role or 44px size

rules.rule: wh-input must use font-size 1rem (>=16px) so iOS does not zoom on focus
rules.metric: computed font-size of .wh-input and text/number/email/password inputs
rules.threshold: >= 16px (skill spec sets .wh-input font-size: 1rem)
rules.detect: JS: getComputedStyle(input).fontSize parsed to px >= 16 for every input.wh-input and editable field

rules.rule: Text/background contrast must clear WCAG AA 4.5:1; the tinted-chip pattern (text + 0.15-0.18 bg in the SAME hue) fails AA for dark hues (red, deep purple, navy) — text must be brightened to a light tint of the hue
rules.metric: axe-core color-contrast ratio on text nodes, especially status chips (.ptype-*, period-pill) and button.danger
rules.threshold: >= 4.5:1 (AA normal text); dark-hue chips like .ptype-shutdown red #E15554-on-tint measured ~3.7-3.9:1 = FAIL
rules.detect: axe rule id 'color-contrast'; additionally JS-compute ratio on .ptype-shutdown, .danger/button.danger, active period/purple pills against composited bg

rules.rule: Muted/ghosted look (empty states, hints, future-step labels) must come from text COLOR, never from container opacity<1 — wrapper opacity composites into descendant text alpha and breaks AA
rules.metric: computed opacity of empty-state/hint/stepper containers AND resulting effective text contrast of descendants
rules.threshold: container opacity == 1 (no opacity<1 on a wrapper holding text); e.g. .empty-state{opacity:0.4} dragged text to 2.2:1 = FAIL
rules.detect: JS: for .empty-state / hint / stepper wrappers, assert getComputedStyle(el).opacity === '1'; flag any text-bearing container with opacity < 1, then re-check descendant contrast via axe color-contrast

rules.rule: Every interactive element inside a modal/bottom-sheet (and globally) must show a visible :focus-visible ring — 2px solid accent, offset 2px (WCAG 2.2 SC 2.4.13)
rules.metric: presence of a :focus-visible outline rule with non-zero width on interactive elements (buttons, inputs, custom toggles)
rules.threshold: outline >= 2px solid, offset 2px, color reads on navy (brand orange #F7A21B 100% for buttons / 70% for input borders); never 0/none, never white outline
rules.detect: JS: focus each interactive el, getComputedStyle(el,':focus-visible') or post-.focus() read outlineWidth>=2px && outlineStyle!=='none'; flag white outline color on dark bg; axe 'focus-visible'-adjacent check

rules.rule: Bottom sheets / modals must carry role="dialog" aria-modal="true" plus aria-labelledby pointing at the sheet title id (Auto-Mineable rule designer_dialog_has_aria_modal_true)
rules.metric: presence of role=dialog + aria-modal=true + aria-labelledby on overlay elements (bottom-sheet / sheet-overlay / modal-overlay)
rules.threshold: all three attributes present; aria-labelledby resolves to an existing element id
rules.detect: DOM querySelector on '.bottom-sheet, .sheet-overlay, .modal-overlay, [class*=overlay]'; assert getAttribute('role')==='dialog' && aria-modal==='true' && document.getElementById(el.getAttribute('aria-labelledby')) exists

rules.rule: Reaction/toggle-style buttons must expose aria-pressed="true|false" and the visual .active class must follow the same value
rules.metric: aria-pressed attribute on toggle buttons (.toggle-btn and reaction toggles) and parity with .active class
rules.threshold: aria-pressed in {true,false} present AND (aria-pressed==='true') === el.classList.contains('active')
rules.detect: JS: for every .toggle-btn / aria-pressed toggle, assert attribute present and boolean parity with the active class

rules.rule: Live-updating regions (connection chips, presence/conn bars, any region that changes without user action) must be ARIA live regions; standard toast role=alert aria-live=polite
rules.metric: presence of role=status aria-live=polite on auto-updating regions; role=alert aria-live=polite on toasts
rules.threshold: role+aria-live present on connection chips (wh-conn-chip), presence bars, status regions, and toast containers
rules.detect: DOM: assert .wh-conn-chip / presence bar / toast nodes have role in {status,alert} && aria-live==='polite'

rules.rule: CTA pulse-ring (and any decorative motion) must be disabled under prefers-reduced-motion
rules.metric: animation on .cta-pulse when prefers-reduced-motion: reduce is active
rules.threshold: animation == none / 'none' under reduced-motion media query
rules.detect: JS: emulate prefers-reduced-motion:reduce, getComputedStyle('.cta-pulse').animationName === 'none'; or assert a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce){ .cta-pulse{animation:none} } rule exists

rules.rule: Bottom-right corner must not stack competing fixed widgets that occlude a page primary-action FAB — no fixed element should leave another interactive fixed element with <44px tappable / mostly covered
rules.metric: overlap of fixed bottom-right elements (#wh-hub, .wh-conn-chip, .wh-feedback-fab, .wh-ai-widget, bottom nav, page FAB) via elementFromPoint at each widget's center
rules.threshold: document.elementFromPoint(centerX,centerY) of each interactive fixed widget returns that widget (not an occluder); visible/tappable region >= 44px (the pm-scheduler FAB left only an 8px sliver = FAIL)
rules.detect: JS: enumerate position:fixed bottom-right els; for each interactive one, call elementFromPoint at its center and at its rect corners; flag if hit element is a different fixed widget or tappable area < 44px

rules.rule: Empty state must present a CTA — Today's One Thing empty state is a green 'all clear' card with a contextual next-step CTA based on role (action-first home pattern)
rules.metric: presence of a primary CTA (button/link) inside an empty-state / 'all clear' card
rules.threshold: empty-state card contains >=1 actionable element (a[href]/button); Today's One Thing always has exactly 1 primary CTA
rules.detect: JS: when a region renders an empty/'all clear' state, assert it querySelector('a[href], button') is non-null; flag empty states with zero CTA

rules.rule: Today's One Thing action card has exactly ONE primary CTA (one .btn-primary), never two competing primary buttons
rules.metric: count of primary CTAs within the Today's One Thing / hero action card
rules.threshold: exactly 1 primary button per action card
rules.detect: JS: scope to the action card container; count .btn-primary (or visually-dominant buttons); assert === 1

rules.rule: Landing hero must use one dominant CTA + one demoted text link — never two equal-weight buttons side by side
rules.metric: count of equal-weight buttons in the hero CTA cluster
rules.threshold: <=1 .btn-primary AND the secondary is a .cta-text-link (text link), not a second button of equal weight
rules.detect: JS: in the hero CTA row, count anchors/buttons styled as buttons (btn-primary/btn-secondary); flag if two button-styled CTAs share equal weight instead of one button + one .cta-text-link

rules.rule: btn-primary must use the canonical orange gradient #F7A21B -> #FDB94A (literal hex or var(--orange-wh)); the drift hex #e8920a is banned
rules.metric: background gradient stops on .btn-primary and absence of #e8920a anywhere
rules.threshold: linear-gradient contains #F7A21B and #FDB94A (or var(--orange-wh)); zero occurrences of #e8920a
rules.detect: JS: getComputedStyle('.btn-primary').backgroundImage matches the orange gradient; scan computed styles / stylesheet text for the banned token e8920a

rules.rule: Input background must be the canonical navy rgba(22,32,50,0.6) (== var(--wh-navy) base), not rgba(0,0,0,0.25) which renders too dark, and card border-radius must be 1rem not 1.25rem
rules.metric: computed background of .wh-input / inputs and border-radius of cards
rules.threshold: input bg == rgba(22,32,50,0.6) (not rgba(0,0,0,...)); card border-radius == 1rem (16px), not 1.25rem
rules.detect: JS: getComputedStyle(input).backgroundColor approx rgba(22,32,50,.6); getComputedStyle('.simple-card / card').borderRadius === '16px'; flag rgba(0,0,0,0.25) inputs and 1.25rem cards

rules.rule: Design tokens: shared components.css :root must define every canonical --wh-* value and real elements must resolve to brand values (e.g. .simple-card bg = rgb(22,32,50) = var(--wh-navy))
rules.metric: presence of --wh-* custom props on :root and computed brand color on a real card
rules.threshold: :root exposes --wh-orange/-blue/-navy(+dark/light), --wh-steel, --wh-cloud, --wh-space-*, --wh-radius*, --wh-font; .simple-card backgroundColor === rgb(22,32,50)
rules.detect: JS: getComputedStyle(document.documentElement).getPropertyValue('--wh-navy') non-empty; getComputedStyle('.simple-card').backgroundColor === 'rgb(22, 32, 50)'

rules.rule: Report tables: header styling must be on each <th>, not on <tr> (tr background/color does not render in doc-panel/report-panel context, making white header text invisible)
rules.metric: background/color declared on <tr> vs <th> within .doc-panel/.report-panel tables, and rendered header readability
rules.threshold: <th> carries the background+color; no header <tr> with inline background/color; header text contrast >= 4.5:1
rules.detect: JS: in report tables, flag any <tr> with non-transparent style.background/color and a child <th> lacking its own background; verify <th> rendered bg is dark and text visible (axe color-contrast on header cells)

rules.rule: PDF/print report DOM must inject page-break protection: tr/td/th/p page-break-inside:avoid+break-inside:avoid, h2/h3 page-break-after:avoid, .result-highlight/.sig-block and the results grid CONTAINER page-break-inside:avoid
rules.metric: presence of injected page-break CSS rules on report elements before html2pdf render
rules.threshold: all listed selectors carry page-break-inside/break-inside:avoid (rows, p, result-highlight, sig-block, grid container) and h2/h3 carry page-break-after:avoid
rules.detect: JS: in print/PDF context, getComputedStyle(report tr/td/th/p).breakInside === 'avoid'; results grid wrapper breakInside === 'avoid'; h2/h3 breakAfter === 'avoid'

rules.rule: Every component state must exist and be reachable: default, hover, active, disabled, empty, loading, error — a design is incomplete if any state is missing (e.g. a list with no empty/loading/error rendering)
rules.basis: skill-rule (Responsibilities / How to Operate: 'Specify every state')
rules.detect: During the journey, drive each component into each state: load with no data (empty), during fetch (loading), on a failed action (error), disable conditions; flag any state that renders blank, broken, or identical to default

rules.rule: Home is mission-control in a strict 3-layer order: Today's One Thing (action) -> Your Stack (context) -> Tools drawer (collapsed behind FAB). Putting tools above context is the 'feels overwhelming' failure mode
rules.basis: skill-rule (Home Stack Pattern, Phase H) / Nielsen-N (aesthetic & minimalist, match system to real world)
rules.detect: On home, verify DOM/visual order is action card first, ranked context cards second, full tool grid collapsed/last; flag if the tool grid appears above context or is expanded by default

rules.rule: Today's One Thing must surface the single highest-priority action ranked across canonical signals (critical risk, PM overdue+critical, out-of-stock, oldest open job) and deep-link to where the action is done; ties demote the loser to a stack card
rules.basis: skill-rule (Home Stack Pattern) / Norman-7 (clear goal, mapping)
rules.detect: With seeded high-priority data, confirm the action card reflects the correct top signal and its CTA deep-links to the right surface; flag wrong/stale ranking or a dead-end CTA

rules.rule: First-time-worker onboarding is a 3-step ladder above the stack, dismissable, and hidden once all steps complete (wh_onboarding_dismissed)
rules.basis: skill-rule (Home Stack Pattern) / Nielsen-N (help & documentation, recognition over recall)
rules.detect: As a fresh worker (no wh_onboarding_dismissed), verify the 3-step ladder shows above the stack and dismisses/hides on completion; flag if it persists after completion or blocks the stack

rules.rule: Role-filtered nav: wh_nav_mode derives from wh_hive_role on first load (worker->field subset, supervisor->supervisor, engineer->engineer) so a new worker doesn't see every tool; existing explicit choices are preserved
rules.basis: skill-rule (Home Stack Pattern) / Nielsen-N (match between system and real world; minimalist)
rules.detect: Sign in per role; confirm the tool drawer shows the role-appropriate subset on first load; flag a new worker seeing the full all-tools grid

rules.rule: Consolidate sibling surfaces: 3+ pages serving one role over the same data (e.g. predictive/shift-brain/ph-intelligence all read v_risk_truth) should hide all but the parent from primary nav with deep-links inside; write-only editors belong as a button on their parent, not a top-level nav entry
rules.basis: skill-rule (When to consolidate sibling surfaces) / Nielsen-N (consistency, minimalist)
rules.detect: Scan primary nav for redundant siblings reading the same data and for write-only editor pages exposed as top-level nav; flag duplication that should be parent+deep-link

rules.rule: For categorical inputs with 3-8 known options, prefer pill toggle button groups over <select> dropdowns (faster to tap on mobile, immediately visible, lower cognitive load); selecting a toggle should auto-fill related numeric fields
rules.basis: skill-rule (Toggle Button Groups) / Nielsen-N (recognition over recall, flexibility & efficiency)
rules.detect: On forms, flag <select> dropdowns with 3-8 fixed options that should be toggle pills; verify selecting a toggle pre-populates dependent numeric fields

rules.rule: Multi-stage tools (4+ input stages) use a step wizard with progressive disclosure: locked steps dimmed + pointer-events:none, active step orange-accented, step label updates ('Step 2 of 4 - ...'); mobile single column, desktop wizard-left/result-right
rules.basis: skill-rule (Step Wizard with Progressive Disclosure) / Nielsen-N (visibility of system status, user control)
rules.detect: On 4+ stage tools, verify steps unlock sequentially, locked steps are non-interactive, the active step is accented, and the step label reflects current position; flag all-steps-open-at-once or missing progress indicator

rules.rule: Editable report fields must be visually signalled as editable on screen (orange dashed underline, focus = light yellow bg) with a tip banner above explaining editability ('Click any underlined field to edit before printing')
rules.basis: skill-rule (Editable Report Fields) / Nielsen-N (visibility of system status, affordance)
rules.detect: On report pages, confirm editable fields carry the dashed-underline affordance + focus treatment and a tip banner explains it; flag invisibly-editable fields with no signifier

rules.rule: Auto-filled (locked-from-lookup) fields must be visually signalled (opacity 0.6, cursor not-allowed, sub-label 'Auto-filled from [standard] - select Custom to override') so the engineer doesn't think the field is broken; restore on Custom
rules.basis: skill-rule (Auto-fill Fields) / Norman-7 (signifiers, mapping; prevent perceived error)
rules.detect: Trigger an auto-fill; verify the field looks disabled with an explanatory sub-label and restores to editable when Custom is chosen; flag a silently-locked field with no explanation

rules.rule: Multi-field domain forms must pair each field with a field tip card that explains the field in plain language, a default/rule-of-thumb when unsure, edge-case behavior, and units/ranges — not merely restating the label
rules.basis: skill-rule (Field Tip Cards) / Nielsen-N (help & documentation, error prevention)
rules.detect: Open the Guide/help panel; verify each domain input has a tip with plain-language meaning + default + units/range; flag tips that just restate the label ('Enter the pipe diameter')

rules.rule: Philippine engineering result highlight boxes must show paired units (kW+TR, kW+kVA, L/s+m3/h, L+m3) in the 2x2 grid — never a single unit alone
rules.basis: skill-rule (Report Dual-Unit Display) / Nielsen-N (match between system and real world)
rules.detect: On engineering result boxes, verify each result shows both required units in adjacent cells; flag any kW/flow/volume value shown without its paired unit

rules.rule: Primary CTA pulse-ring is for the hero CTA ONLY (motion on the periphery, not inside the button) — do not apply .cta-pulse to every btn-primary across the platform
rules.basis: skill-rule (CTA Pulse-Ring) / Nielsen-N (aesthetic & minimalist; avoid distraction)
rules.detect: Count animated .cta-pulse elements per page; flag if pulse animation is applied to non-hero buttons or many buttons at once

rules.rule: Social proof (waitlist count + avatar row) should sit immediately below the hero CTA, not buried only at the bottom join section
rules.basis: skill-rule (Social Proof) / Nielsen-N (credibility / persuasion)
rules.detect: On the landing page, check whether a compact social-proof row appears near the hero CTA; flag if social proof exists only at the page bottom

rules.rule: Provenance / trust / lineage UI must be authored in USER voice (the reader's mental model, e.g. 'Live - Based on your logbook & inventory'), never leaking internals (DB view names v_*_truth, RPC/edge-fn names, function identifiers, .md refs, raw SQL); humanize the visible label, keep the stored value/enum
rules.basis: skill-rule (Provenance UI must be in user voice, STREAMLINE E1) / Nielsen-N (match system to real world; speak the user's language) / WCAG-POUR (Understandable)
rules.detect: Read every KPI source-chip / provenance / lineage caption on the live page; flag any string exposing view names, function/RPC names, code identifiers, doc filenames, or raw SQL instead of plain-language source labels

rules.rule: Tinted-status-chip color must preserve semantic identity while clearing AA: brighten the TEXT to a light tint of the hue (red #E15554->#F58A89, purple #a78bfa->#c4b5fd); do NOT darken the bg (kills the tint look) or desaturate to grey (loses the meaning)
rules.basis: skill-rule (tinted-chip dark-hue lesson, battery B3) / WCAG-POUR (Perceivable, contrast) / Nielsen-N (consistency, recognition)
rules.detect: For each failing dark-hue chip flagged by contrast, verify the remedy keeps the hue identity (still reads red/purple) rather than turning grey or losing the tint; flag fixes that destroy semantic color or the tint pattern

rules.rule: When designing/placing any new floating affordance (FAB, chip), it must declare WHERE in the bottom-right corner stack it sits and what it must clear; page actions should route into the nav-hub rather than spawning a competing FAB
rules.basis: skill-rule (bottom-right corner widget-priority gap) / Nielsen-N (consistency & standards) / Norman-7 (constraints, no interference)
rules.detect: On any page that adds a primary-action FAB, confirm it has a defined slot/gap in the corner order and does not collide with shared widgets (nav-hub, conn-chip, feedback FAB, AI widget, bottom nav); flag an ad-hoc FAB with no stacking budget

rules.rule: Imported/auto-filled/AI-suggested data must give the user visible control: an editable checkbox row to uncheck before merge, a colored provenance chip per item (Skill Matrix / Logbook / Uploaded / AI suggested / You typed), and an Undo control — turning 'the AI changed my stuff' anxiety into trust
rules.basis: skill-rule (Resume/CV Builder lesson) / Nielsen-N (user control & freedom, visibility of system status) / Norman-7 (feedback, conceptual model)
rules.detect: On any auto-fill / upload / AI-suggestion flow, verify each incoming item is an editable, source-tagged, uncheckable row with an Undo affordance before it merges; flag silent merges with no provenance or undo

rules.rule: Brand consistency: stay within the platform palette (orange/blue/navy/steel/cloud), Poppins type, and canonical radii (1rem cards / 0.75rem inputs / 999px pills) — don't introduce new colors or fonts without strong reason
rules.basis: skill-rule (Design System / How to Operate: stay within the brand) / Nielsen-N (consistency & standards)
rules.detect: Scan the live page for off-brand colors/fonts and non-canonical radii on cards/inputs/pills; flag ad-hoc hues, non-Poppins fonts, or drifted border-radii

rules.rule: Mobile-first: every surface must be designed and usable at 375px first, then scale up to desktop (1280px) — no horizontal overflow, no clipped/unreachable controls at narrow width
rules.basis: skill-rule (How to Operate: Mobile first; Specify responsive behavior) / WCAG-POUR (Operable, reflow 1.4.10) / Nielsen-N (flexibility & efficiency)
rules.detect: Render at 375px and inspect for horizontal scroll, overlapping/occluded controls, or content that requires desktop width; flag layouts that break or hide actions on mobile

rules.rule: Focus-ring color must read against the navy theme: brand orange #F7A21B (100% for buttons, 70% variant for input borders); never white outlines (they blur against the slate-on-navy gradient)
rules.basis: skill-rule (Bottom-Sheet visual focus-ring requirement) / WCAG-POUR (Perceivable, focus visibility) / Nielsen-N (visibility of system status)
rules.detect: Tab through interactive elements; visually confirm focus outline is brand-orange and clearly visible on the dark bg; flag white/low-contrast focus rings

rules.rule: Article/figure visuals are mobile-first and annotation-first: vertical flows beat horizontal (a 5-step horizontal row is unreadable at 390px), orange number badges carry scan order, keep simple for a plant-floor (non-stats-literate) audience, and every figure with external numbers carries a 'Source:' caption (figures obey the truth doctrine)
rules.basis: skill-rule (Brand figure spec for learn-article SVGs) / Nielsen-N (match real world, aesthetic & minimalist) / WCAG-POUR (Understandable)
rules.detect: On article/learn pages, check figures use vertical layout at mobile width, readable labels (>=9px effective), numbered scan order, and a Source caption when showing external numbers; flag dense horizontal charts or unsourced external-number figures

rules.rule: Interactive/tap targets must be at least 44x44px (platform floor: 44px everywhere, no exceptions, including filter-chips and buttons rendered inside JS template literals).
rules.metric: min(boundingRect.width, boundingRect.height) of every interactive element (a, button, [role=button], [onclick], input[type=button/submit], .toggle-btn, .filter-chip, chips, FABs, nav-hub/companion controls)
rules.threshold: >= 44px on both width and height
rules.detect: JS: for each el matching 'a,button,[role=button],[onclick],input,select,textarea,.toggle-btn,.kw-chip,.seed-btn,.chip', r=el.getBoundingClientRect(); fail if Math.min(r.width,r.height) < 44. Cross-check with axe-core rule id 'target-size' (WCAG 2.5.8). Note: a 56px position:fixed FAB passes; re-measure the actual element before trusting axe 'incomplete'.

rules.rule: Tap-target fix must actually land at runtime: on Tailwind-utility pages min-h-[44px] can silently no-op in a compiled build; the measured height must still be >=44px (inline style or base button rule).
rules.metric: computed min-height / measured height of buttons after fix
rules.threshold: >= 44px measured (not just class present)
rules.detect: JS: getBoundingClientRect().height >= 44 AND getComputedStyle(el).minHeight resolves to >=44px; verify the class actually compiled (don't trust presence of min-h-[44px]).

rules.rule: Form/modal text inputs must be at least 16px font-size so iOS Safari does not auto-zoom on focus.
rules.metric: computed font-size on input, textarea, select, [contenteditable], especially inside modals (Tailwind text-sm=14px is the usual culprit)
rules.detect: JS: for each 'input,textarea,select,[contenteditable="true"]', parseFloat(getComputedStyle(el).fontSize) >= 16. Scope a check to modal inputs (#modal input, #signin-modal input).

rules.rule: Normal text must meet WCAG 1.4.3 AA contrast; the dark-theme muted/secondary-text floor is white-alpha 0.6 (page bg) and ~0.85 on raised --surface cards. rgba(255,255,255,<=0.4) (and 0.3/0.35/0.5) always fails.
rules.metric: color-contrast ratio of rendered text vs its actual background
rules.threshold: >= 4.5:1 for normal text (>= 3:1 large text >=18.66px bold / >=24px). White@0.6 ~= 6.8:1 passes; white@0.4 ~= 3.3-4:1 fails; raised cards need ~0.85.
rules.detect: axe-core rule id 'color-contrast' run with resultTypes:['violations'] ~2s after the page settles. Also static grep for color:rgba(255,255,255,0.[1-5]) in HTML and JS render templates (latent siblings in collapsed/empty states only render under specific data).

rules.rule: Never mute a text block via opacity<1 on the element or any ancestor — CSS opacity multiplies into descendant text alpha, so a child color:0.6 inside a container opacity:0.4 composites to 0.24 effective and fails AA even though the node's own color looks fine.
rules.metric: product of opacity along the ancestor chain of any element wrapping readable text (steady-state, not :disabled/keyframes/decorative-svg)
rules.threshold: effective text alpha must still yield >= 4.5:1; container opacity should be 1 for text wrappers
rules.detect: Static grep -nE 'opacity:\s*0?\.[1-7]|opacity-[1-6]0' over *.html, then for each hit walk DOM ancestors: fail if any ancestor of a text node has computed opacity<1 (exempt :disabled, @keyframes/skeleton/pulse, decorative <svg>/glow blobs, *-test/backup/admin pages). When axe reports a ratio far worse than the element's own color implies, walk the ancestor chain for opacity.

rules.rule: A page must have exactly one <h1> landmark in the LIVE DOM (not only in source / template strings). Gates/overlays/empty-states that innerHTML-replace a region wipe the page's <h1>, so the runtime h1 count can be 0 even when source greps pass.
rules.metric: document.querySelectorAll('h1').length after page/gate/empty-state settles
rules.threshold: === 1 (must be >= 1)
rules.detect: JS after load + after any maturity-gate/empty-state renders: document.querySelectorAll('h1').length; axe rule id 'page-has-heading-one'. Beware titles rendered as <div/span class="page-title"> = 0 real h1, and template-string h1s that never reach the live DOM.

rules.rule: Every form control needs a programmatic accessible name (a placeholder is NOT a label). Applies to JS-template-built <select> and to the .field/.wh-input visible-but-unassociated <label> pattern.
rules.metric: presence of accessible name: <label for> + matching id, OR aria-label, OR wrapping <label>, on every input/select/textarea
rules.threshold: 100% of form controls have a non-empty accessible name
rules.detect: axe-core rule ids 'label' and 'select-name' (live run catches JS-template selects a static grep misses). JS: each control has computed accName != '' (htmlFor link, aria-label, or wrapping label). Derive input type from field key (email/tel/number) for correct mobile keyboard.

rules.rule: Toast/status containers must carry role="alert" and aria-live="polite" or they are silent to screen readers.
rules.metric: presence of role and aria-live on the toast/status region (#toast, undo toast, connection chip)
rules.threshold: role="alert" AND aria-live="polite" present (status regions: aria-live="polite")
rules.detect: JS/axe: el#toast.getAttribute('role')==='alert' && getAttribute('aria-live')==='polite'. Reference impl: skillmatrix.html toast.

rules.rule: Filter UIs built as tabs/chips must declare role="tablist" on the container and role="tab"+aria-selected on each button; ARIA tabs must keep aria-selected in sync in the switch handler, not only in markup.
rules.metric: ARIA roles/state on filter containers (.section-tabs/.filter-row/.filter-tabs/.chip-row/.tab-row) and their buttons; aria-selected reflects the currently-active tab after switching
rules.threshold: container role=tablist; each tab role=tab + aria-controls; exactly one aria-selected=true matching the visually active tab; panels role=tabpanel + aria-labelledby
rules.detect: JS: container.getAttribute('role')==='tablist'; buttons have role='tab'; after a tab click, the [aria-selected=true] tab == the visually active tab. axe 'aria-required-children'/'aria-allowed-attr'.

rules.rule: A modal/dialog must expose role="dialog" + aria-modal="true" + a non-empty accessible name (aria-label or aria-labelledby pointing to filled text), ESC-to-close, a Tab/Shift+Tab focus trap, and focus-restore to the opener.
rules.metric: dialog ARIA attributes + accessible name resolves non-empty in the CLOSED scan state + ESC handler + focus trap presence
rules.threshold: role=dialog AND aria-modal=true AND accName != '' (add static aria-label fallback so a JS-filled aria-labelledby isn't empty when closed)
rules.detect: axe rule id 'aria-dialog-name'. JS: dialog has role=dialog/aria-modal; computed accName non-empty even before JS fills the title; press ESC -> dialog closes; Tab from last focusable wraps to first; on close document.activeElement === opener.

rules.rule: Hidden interactive containers must be made inert (not only aria-hidden): toggling aria-hidden on a slide-away CTA/region leaves its <a>/<button> Tab-focusable (axe aria-hidden-focus, WCAG 4.1.2).
rules.metric: any focusable descendant of an aria-hidden / visually-hidden container that remains in the tab order
rules.threshold: 0 focusable elements reachable inside an aria-hidden/off-screen container (container must have inert set)
rules.detect: axe rule id 'aria-hidden-focus'. JS: for each [aria-hidden=true] or display:none-but-tabbable container, assert no descendant a/button/input/[tabindex>=0] is focusable AND container.inert === true.

rules.rule: Clickable non-button elements (div/span with onclick) must be keyboard-operable: role="button" + tabindex="0" + a keydown handler (Enter/Space -> click) + a :focus-visible outline. A visible text label does NOT make a div keyboard-operable.
rules.metric: for every element with an onclick that is not a native button/a/input: presence of role=button, tabindex=0, keyboard activation, and a visible focus indicator
rules.threshold: all four present on every clickable non-button element
rules.detect: JS: querySelectorAll('[onclick]'):tagName not in (BUTTON,A,INPUT) -> must have role=button && tabindex=='0'; check a focusable :focus-visible rule exists (e.g. .x-clickable:focus-visible{outline...}). Maps to L3 battery clickAudit 'click:not-keyboard-operable'. WCAG 2.1.1 + 2.4.7.

rules.rule: A focus-visible indicator must be present on interactive elements; pairing outline:none with no :focus state hides the cursor/ring (especially contenteditable fields).
rules.metric: presence of a visible :focus / :focus-visible style (outline, background, or border change) on focusable elements that set outline:none
rules.threshold: every element with outline:none has a non-default :focus/:focus-visible visual state
rules.detect: Inspect document.styleSheets for a :focus or :focus-visible rule paired with each outline:none rule; live: focus the element via keyboard and assert a computed style change (outline/background/border).

rules.rule: A page FAB / page-added fixed control must not be occluded by the stacked bottom-right widget column (nav-hub FAB z9998, connectivity chip z9998 bottom:5.5rem, feedback FAB, AI widget z9999, bottom-nav). Two action FABs in one corner is the single-FAB-hub anti-pattern.
rules.metric: at the visual center of any page .fab, the topmost element (elementFromPoint) is the FAB itself, and its usable (non-overlapped) tap area is still >=44px
rules.threshold: FAB is hit-testable (elementFromPoint == FAB) with >=44px usable area; not partially obscured
rules.detect: At 390px width: r=fab.getBoundingClientRect(); document.elementFromPoint(r.left+r.width/2, r.top+r.height/2) === fab (or a descendant). axe 'target-size' flags 'partially obscured'. Check bottom-right occupancy for >1 action FAB.

rules.rule: Multi-column data tables must not force document-width overflow at 360px; wide tables (>=5 columns / wide content) need a scroll-in-card wrapper (overflow-x:auto) so the table scrolls inside its card rather than pushing the page.
rules.metric: document horizontal overflow at narrow viewport
rules.threshold: document.documentElement.scrollWidth <= clientWidth at 360px (no horizontal page scroll)
rules.detect: browser_resize(360, h) then assert documentElement.scrollWidth <= documentElement.clientWidth (the U7 probe). Tables with >=5 columns must sit inside <div style=overflow-x:auto> or @media(max-width:600px){table{display:block;overflow-x:auto}}.

rules.rule: A data list page must distinguish empty-state (no data) from no-results (filter matched nothing) and must show a loading state during fetch and an error state on failure — each with a dedicated anchor/affordance, not a blank panel or a stuck spinner.
rules.metric: presence of #empty-state, #no-results (for filterable lists), a loading skeleton/anchor, and an error/retry affordance
rules.threshold: empty-state present; no-results present when a search input exists; loading anchor present (or whListSkeleton call); error path present (whListError/role=alert + Retry)
rules.detect: JS/static: querySelector('#empty-state'), '#no-results', a '.skeleton/.wh-skeleton/[aria-busy]' or whListSkeleton( call, and a whListError(/role=alert error block. Retry control must be >=44px. List View Contract rule ids frontend_list_view_has_empty_state / _no_results_state.

rules.rule: No element may remain stuck on a 'Loading…' / 'Computing…' placeholder after the page settles, even with zero console errors (missing CDN, uncalled renderer, empty/error branch that skips a placeholder, or unguarded HIVE_ID query).
rules.metric: count of residual 'Loading' / 'Computing' / spinner text in document.body.innerText after load settles (~7s)
rules.threshold: 0 residual loading/computing placeholders after settle
rules.detect: After ~7s: (document.body.innerText.match(/Loading\.\.\.|Computing/gi)||[]).length === 0. Combine with console-clean check (a non-zero count with 0 errors = stuck-placeholder render gap). Also assert @supabase/supabase-js CDN <script src> precedes utils.js and client-getter matches getDb.

rules.rule: Page fade-in animations on <body> must never leave the page permanently invisible: requires a prefers-reduced-motion override and a JS opacity safety timeout.
rules.metric: computed body opacity after load under reduced-motion and after the safety window
rules.threshold: body opacity === 1 after load (animation must not strand body at opacity:0); reduced-motion -> animation:none
rules.detect: JS: getComputedStyle(document.body).opacity === '1' after ~800-1000ms; verify @media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){body{animation:none}} exists in styleSheets and an inline 800ms guard sets opacity:1. Body animations must be opacity-only (never transform/filter/clip-path — those break position:fixed).

rules.rule: A count/total badge that labels a list it does not physically contain must equal the underlying list (annotate with data-count-of / data-sum-of so it is machine-checkable); the rendered integer must match the actual row count.
rules.metric: rendered count integer vs querySelectorAll(data-count-of within data-count-scope).length (or Σ of data-sum-col across rows for sums)
rules.threshold: rendered value === computed count/sum (within data-sum-tol for sums)
rules.detect: JS: for [data-count-of], parseInt(el.textContent) === document.querySelectorAll(el.dataset.countScope+' '+el.dataset.countOf).length. Litmus: any 'N entries / N open / N total' badge must come from a COUNT query, not array.length of a .limit()-ed fetch.

rules.rule: Negative 'days until / time remaining / countdown' values must render as 'overdue', never a literal minus sign.
rules.metric: any countdown/days-until value that is rendered with a leading '-' (minus)
rules.threshold: 0 rendered negative-minus countdowns (negative -> 'Nd overdue')
rules.detect: Regex scan rendered text for /-\d+\s*(d|day|days|min|hr)/ in countdown/forecast/'days until' contexts; if a minus sign appears the formatter is buggy.

rules.rule: Every page should declare a canonical <link rel="canonical"> in <head> (SEO/equity; flagged missing on several pages).
rules.metric: presence of link[rel=canonical] in head
rules.threshold: exactly one link[rel=canonical] present
rules.detect: JS: document.querySelector('link[rel="canonical"]') !== null.

rules.rule: article cards must not use role="listitem" (invalid for <article>); use role="feed" on the container instead.
rules.metric: invalid ARIA role nesting on card grids (article role=listitem)
rules.threshold: 0 invalid role combinations
rules.detect: axe rule id 'aria-allowed-role' / 'aria-required-parent'. JS: no article[role=listitem]; card grid container uses role=feed with aria-label.

rules.rule: Empty states must be written for the WEAKEST starting point and offer every path forward (not assume pre-existing data); first-run guidance must key on actual user-entered CONTENT, not on identity fields the app pre-fills.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Help & documentation / Recognition) + skill-rule (novice-user robustness, resume empty-state)
rules.detect: On a fresh/empty account, check the empty state names the action AND presents all entry paths (auto-fill / upload / type). Verify guidance appears for a brand-new user (it must not be gated on a pre-filled name). A blank panel with no CTA = fail.

rules.rule: An empty/error branch must reset and repopulate EVERY placeholder the success branch would have set — including cards in sibling containers and renderers that are defined but never called — so no stale spinner survives.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Visibility of system status) + skill-rule (reset-every-placeholder / defined-but-uncalled renderer)
rules.detect: After triggering the empty/error path, scan for any element still showing its static Loading…/Computing… placeholder; confirm the success-path renderer is actually invoked, not just defined.

rules.rule: Hero numbers must show the actual value (a number or a clear status word like HEALTHY); direction labels (INCREASING/DECREASING/STABLE) belong in the sublabel with an arrow, never as the big number.
rules.basis: Norman-N (mapping / match between system and real world) + skill-rule (Hero Number)
rules.detect: On dashboards/KPI cards, read the largest/hero text per card; flag any whose hero is a bare direction word instead of a count/status. The trend/direction should be the smaller sublabel.

rules.rule: Maintenance KPI trend charts must keep 0 at the bottom (more=worse, less=better); never let an auto-inverted Y-axis make a falling failure/downtime/MTTR trend look like it is rising.
rules.basis: WCAG-POUR (Understandable) + Norman-N (good mapping) + skill-rule (Plotly Y-axis auto-invert)
rules.detect: For any decreasing time series, confirm the y-axis is not inverted (0 at bottom, max at top). A chart where an improving trend slopes upward = misleading.

rules.rule: Long flat lists (50+ rows) must be grouped by their natural parent entity with progressive disclosure: sort groups by urgency, auto-expand the highest-urgency (overdue) group, collapse the rest, and show count + worst-badge on each collapsed header.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Aesthetic & minimalist design / Recognition) + skill-rule (Long Flat Lists)
rules.detect: On any page rendering 50+ items, check it is grouped + collapsible, urgency-sorted, overdue group auto-expanded, and collapsed headers state count + worst status. A raw 50+ row table on mobile = fail.

rules.rule: Every FK link / linked entity captured on a form must appear in the save/confirmation summary, so the user can see the link was not silently dropped (especially optional fields they filled).
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Visibility of system status / Error prevention) + skill-rule (Confirmation Feedback for Linked Entities)
rules.detect: Complete a form that links an asset/inventory/order ref, submit, and confirm the summary/confirmation screen displays each captured link. A captured-but-unshown link = fail.

rules.rule: After revealing dynamically-shown content (calc result, BOM/SOW panel, opened sheet) on mobile, scroll it into view; content appearing below the fold reads as 'nothing happened'.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Visibility of system status) + Norman-N (feedback) + skill-rule (Dynamic Result Panels — Scroll Into View)
rules.detect: On a <768px viewport, trigger an action that reveals content below the fold; confirm the view scrolls to the new content. If the result stays off-screen with no scroll, flag it.

rules.rule: Never show a success toast / close a modal before the DB write is confirmed; on write failure the optimistic cache and UI must revert so the UI never claims success the DB didn't persist.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Error prevention / Visibility of status) + Norman-N (feedback truthfulness) + skill-rule (Local Cache Must Revert on DB Write Failure)
rules.detect: Force a write failure (offline / bad input) and confirm the UI shows an error (not a success toast), the modal stays open or reopens, and the optimistic change is rolled back. A success toast on a failed/lost write = blocker.

rules.rule: Destructive/irreversible actions a novice can fat-finger (e.g. row-remove ✕) must offer recovery (undo) — every destructive mutation path, no handler exempt.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (User control & freedom — undo/redo) + skill-rule (every destructive mutation pushes undo)
rules.detect: Trigger each delete/remove affordance and confirm an Undo (or equivalent recovery) actually works for that specific action — a visible Undo button that no-ops on the most-used delete = fail.

rules.rule: A header that says 'Your X' (or any honest-stat label) must never read 0/stale when the user actually has rows; counts must reflect the user's own data in every view/role default.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Match between system & real world / Visibility) + skill-rule (honest header stats / Calm-Dashboard lie)
rules.detect: As each role (esp. supervisor default view), check 'Your …' header pills show the user's real counts, not 0. A label claiming personal ownership while showing 0 over populated data = fail.

rules.rule: A bare '0' and an 'N/A —' are different truths: when a tile's value depends on a link/join that may be absent, render an honest em-dash with a tooltip ('Not linked yet'), never a 0 that conflates 'none' with 'not applicable'.
rules.basis: Norman-N (match between system & real world) + WCAG-POUR (Understandable) + skill-rule (bare 0 vs N/A —)
rules.detect: For tiles backed by an optional link/join, verify an absent link renders '—' (with explanatory tooltip), not 0. A 0 where the relationship is simply not configured = misleading.

rules.rule: For optional/compliance fields not supplied, render a clear grey 'Not provided — do X to enable' informational state (never blank, never NaN/0, never a FAIL) and still render the summary card placeholder so the grid doesn't break.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Help users recognize/recover / Aesthetic consistency) + skill-rule (Optional Compliance Fields 'Not Provided' pattern)
rules.detect: Leave an optional input empty and generate the report; confirm a grey 'Not provided' note that explains how to enable the check (not a fail color, not a missing card, not 'undefined').

rules.rule: Multi-mode / multi-calc tools must show context-sensitive guidance/help that updates when the active mode changes; a static guide covering only one mode shows irrelevant or misleading content to users in another mode.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Help & documentation / Consistency) + skill-rule (Dynamic Tab Content GUIDES pattern)
rules.detect: Switch modes/calc types and confirm the guide/help tab content changes to match the active mode (formulas, field tips, standards). Stale help for the current mode = fail.

rules.rule: Auto-filled-from-standards/lookup fields must be visibly distinguished (read-only + dimmed) so the user knows the value is derived and cannot silently overwrite a code-mandated value; a 'Custom' option lifts the lock.
rules.basis: Norman-N (constraints / signifiers) + Nielsen-N (Error prevention) + skill-rule (Auto-fill + Read-only pattern)
rules.detect: Pick a lookup-driven option and confirm the dependent numeric field becomes read-only and dimmed (opacity ~0.6); choose Custom and confirm it becomes editable at full opacity.

rules.rule: Input field labels should quantify the impact of a high-stakes choice (e.g. orientation/insulation toggles that change the result by >2x) so users understand the consequence before choosing.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Help users / Visibility) + skill-rule (New Input Field Hint — Quantify the Impact)
rules.detect: For toggles whose options change a computed result by >2x, check the label hint states the magnitude of difference. Absence is a polish gap, not a break.

rules.rule: Mode-irrelevant input sections must be hidden (display:none) when not applicable, and toggle buttons that control field visibility must actually update visibility (call the visibility helper, not just set the value) so dependent fields appear/disappear correctly.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Aesthetic & minimalist / Match to task) + skill-rule (Mode-Dependent Section Visibility / Toggle-Controlled Field Visibility)
rules.detect: Switch each mode/toggle and confirm dependent field groups show/hide correctly and the default hidden state matches the default-active toggle. A toggle that sets a value but never reveals its dependent field = fail.

rules.rule: Categorical inputs with 3-8 options should use toggle button groups (and browsable value lists a chip/tab picker) rather than a native <select>, for faster mobile+desktop selection; already-used options are filtered out of pickers.
rules.basis: Norman-N (affordance / efficiency) + Nielsen-N (Flexibility & efficiency of use) + skill-rule (Toggle Button Groups / Replace Native Select With Chip Picker)
rules.detect: Review categorical inputs: 3-8 fixed options behind a tiny native select (vs a toggle group), or a browsable list trapped in a select, is a usability/polish improvement candidate.

rules.rule: Field-confirmation flows that need extra input should use a thumb-reachable bottom sheet (max-height 80vh, scrollable, backdrop-tap = cancel), not a new screen or a top modal, keeping context visible behind it.
rules.basis: skill-rule (Bottom Sheet Pattern for Field Confirmation) + Nielsen-N (Match to task / context retention)
rules.detect: For in-flow additional-input steps on mobile, check it presents as a bottom sheet (thumb zone, context visible) with Cancel + backdrop-dismiss; a full navigation away or a centered desktop-style modal on mobile is a downgrade.

rules.rule: Editable report fields (contenteditable) must signal editability: visible affordance (dashed underline), text cursor on hover, a focus state, and a one-line tip ('Click any underlined field to edit'); print/PDF strips the dashes.
rules.basis: Norman-N (signifiers / discoverability) + Nielsen-N (Visibility) + skill-rule (Editable Report / contenteditable Focus Feedback)
rules.detect: On editable reports, confirm fields look editable (dashed border), show a text cursor and a focus highlight, and a tip banner explains the affordance; an invisible editable field = discoverability fail.

rules.rule: Physics/formula result steps in engineering reports must show the full derivation (formula name+standard, symbolic formula, each variable with value/unit, substituted intermediate, bold final), not just the final number, so a reviewer can verify it.
rules.basis: WCAG-POUR (Understandable) + domain skill-rule (Physics Formula Steps — Show the Work) + Maintenance/engineering review standards
rules.detect: In a generated engineering report, check each physics step shows the worked derivation, not only an answer; a single computed number with no shown work fails PRC/client-review standards.

rules.rule: Em dashes must not appear in AI-prompt template strings or rendered AI documents (they double-encode to 'â€"' garble on Windows/UTF-8 misread); user-facing AI output must be free of encoding garble.
rules.basis: WCAG-POUR (Perceivable/Understandable) + skill-rule (No Em Dashes in AI Prompt Template Strings)
rules.detect: Scan rendered AI BOM/SOW/narrative text for the fingerprint 'â€' or stray em-dash garble; presence indicates a double-encoded em dash from a prompt template.

rules.rule: AI narrative objects ({objective, assumptions, recommendations}) must be rendered as separate labeled sections, never passed whole to a template/escHtml (which renders the literal '[object Object]' to the user).
rules.basis: skill-rule (Narrative Object — Always Render Fields Separately) + Nielsen-N (error prevention in output)
rules.detect: Scan rendered report/AI panels for the literal text '[object Object]' or a single unlabeled narrative blob; each of objective/assumptions/recommendations should appear in its own labeled block.

rules.rule: A compliance/status result card must reflect the true PASS/FAIL state (the boolean gate must match the exact sentinel the backend returns) — a card that always shows the FAIL/REVIEW color on valid passing input is a silent correctness lie.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Match between system & real world / Visibility of true status) + skill-rule (Result String Sentinel)
rules.detect: Run a calc/check with valid passing inputs and confirm the result card actually turns to the PASS state/color; a card stuck on FAIL/REVIEW despite valid input = fail.

rules.rule: Deep links must land the user on the OPENED detail/applied-filter, not merely a loaded page; input-handoff deep links (e.g. ?q=) must prefill+focus only and never auto-submit an AI call or write (zero-click action risk).
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (User control & freedom / Match) + WCAG-POUR + skill-rule (deep-link reader patterns) + security
rules.detect: Follow each emitted deep-link param and confirm the destination opens the intended detail/filters correctly (not a default page); confirm ?q=-style links prefill and focus but do NOT auto-fire an AI request or write.

rules.rule: Connection/online-offline and live-status indicators must be visible and truthful (offline banner with pending count for field workers; realtime boards reflect INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE so status changes and deletions are not invisible until reload).
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Visibility of system status) + skill-rule (IndexedDB Offline Queue / Realtime Subscribe to All Three Events)
rules.detect: Go offline and submit: confirm a visible offline banner + pending badge + 'saved offline' message; on a live board, change a record's status and delete one elsewhere and confirm the board updates without a manual reload.

rules.rule: Toggle/visual state must stay in sync with underlying state after resets (form.reset() does not fire change events): a custom pill/toggle/status indicator must be manually re-synced so it never shows a 'frozen' selection that ignores clicks.
rules.basis: Norman-N (system state visibility / feedback) + skill-rule (form.reset() — Always Follow With Visual State Reset)
rules.detect: Reset/clear a form with custom toggles and confirm the visual toggles match the reset state; then click the apparently-active option and confirm it responds (a toggle that looks active but does nothing on click = fail).

rules.rule: Cross-card / cross-page numbers about the same set must agree; denominators/totals must derive from the canonical source, not hardcoded — two numbers on one screen about the same set disagreeing signals a hardcode or wrong filter.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Consistency & standards / Match to real world) + skill-rule (Derive denominators from canonical source / cross-surface KPI parity)
rules.detect: On a page/loop, compare numbers that describe the same set (e.g. '3 of 5' vs '×6 disciplines', overdue count vs row count, header total vs board total); any disagreement = a correctness defect to flag.

rules.rule: All tap targets >= 44x44px (no exceptions, including JS-template-literal buttons, icon-only buttons, summary toggles, chips, pills, tabs, FABs)
rules.metric: min(boundingClientRect.width, boundingClientRect.height) on every interactive element
rules.threshold: >= 44px (both width and height)
rules.detect: Enumerate live DOM els matching a,button,[role=button],[role=tab],input[type=button],.chip,.pill,.filter-chip,.btn-icon,.fab,summary; for each visible (el.checkVisibility()) read getBoundingClientRect(); assert width>=44 && height>=44. Measure the COMPUTED rect, not the class. A small control inside a >=44 row hitbox (li/tr/.row) passes. Measure at confirmed CSS 375/390 width (account for devicePixelRatio: confirm window.innerWidth==target after resize, else resize to target*dpr).

rules.rule: Text-entry inputs must have font-size >= 16px (iOS Safari auto-zoom threshold is exactly 16px/1rem)
rules.metric: getComputedStyle(el).fontSize on text-entry fields only (input[type=text|email|number|search|tel|url|password|date], typeless input, textarea, select)
rules.threshold: >= 16px (1rem)
rules.detect: Enumerate input/textarea/select from the LIVE DOM AFTER every panel/dialog/draft is rendered (runtime-injected fields bypass .wh-input baseline); filter to text-entry types (exclude radio/checkbox/range/color/file/button - they never zoom); read computed font-size; assert >=16. Catches Tailwind text-sm/text-xs (14px/12px) and inline style='font-size:0.875rem' overriding the .wh-input 1rem base.

rules.rule: Color contrast must meet WCAG (high contrast important for bright/outdoor plant-floor use)
rules.metric: axe-core color-contrast rule
rules.threshold: >= 4.5:1 (normal text) per axe color-contrast
rules.detect: Run axe-core; check rule id 'color-contrast' has zero violations.

rules.rule: No horizontal scroll on any page at 375/390px
rules.metric: document.documentElement.scrollWidth - clientWidth
rules.threshold: === 0 (no horizontal overflow)
rules.detect: At confirmed CSS 375/390 width (verify window.innerWidth==target after resize, account for dpr), assert document.documentElement.scrollWidth - document.documentElement.clientWidth === 0. Watch for inline grid-template-columns with fixed px columns and fixed-width pill rows of 4+ items.

rules.rule: Toggle buttons / pill option selectors must be >= 44px tall on mobile
rules.metric: getComputedStyle(el).height (computed) on .toggle-btn / pill option selectors at 375px viewport
rules.threshold: >= 44px height
rules.detect: At 375px viewport measure computed height of any .toggle-btn / pill-style option selector; padding:0.5rem alone yields ~40px (fails). Assert >=44.

rules.rule: Visual checkbox/check-box indicator must be glove-visible even when the row is the hitbox
rules.metric: boundingClientRect of the visual .check-box indicator element
rules.threshold: visual indicator >= 32x32px; hitbox (row) >= 44x44px
rules.detect: For checklist-style items, measure both the visual indicator box (.check-box: must be >=32x32) and the wrapping tappable row (.check-item: must be >=44x44 effective hitbox).

rules.rule: viewport meta must include viewport-fit=cover (else env(safe-area-inset-*) returns 0)
rules.metric: viewport meta content attribute
rules.threshold: must contain 'viewport-fit=cover'
rules.detect: Read document.querySelector('meta[name=viewport]').content; assert it includes 'viewport-fit=cover'. Check each page individually (viewport tags are not shared).

rules.rule: Floating AI widget / mobile bottom-fixed elements must clear env(safe-area-inset-bottom)
rules.metric: CSS bottom value of #wh-ai-widget (and sticky bottom bars) in the mobile media query
rules.threshold: bottom must use max(16px, env(safe-area-inset-bottom)); not a bare fixed bottom:16px
rules.detect: Inspect computed/declared bottom of #wh-ai-widget and any position:fixed bottom:0 sticky CTA bar at <=480px; assert it resolves to max(16px, env(safe-area-inset-bottom)) (requires viewport-fit=cover). Bare bottom:16px overlaps the iPhone home indicator.

rules.rule: manifest.json must exist and be linked for PWA installability
rules.metric: presence of <link rel=manifest> and a fetchable manifest with name, icons (192 & 512), start_url, display:standalone
rules.threshold: manifest present + required keys present; icons include 192x192 and 512x512 with purpose 'any maskable'
rules.detect: Assert document.querySelector('link[rel=manifest]') exists; fetch it and assert name, start_url, display==='standalone', icons sizes 192x192 and 512x512.

rules.rule: A focusable focus ring (:focus-visible) must replace any zeroed outline
rules.metric: presence of a :focus-visible outline rule when a control rule sets outline:none
rules.threshold: if outline:none is declared, a matching :focus-visible {outline:2px solid ...} rule must exist
rules.detect: Scan <style> blocks (NOT full file text) for outline:none on interactive controls; assert a corresponding select:focus-visible/button:focus-visible {outline:...} rule exists. Battery flags 'focus-miss' otherwise.

rules.rule: Page must have a real <header> banner landmark (a div.page-header is not a landmark)
rules.metric: presence of a <header> element
rules.threshold: exactly one <header> banner landmark element present
rules.detect: Assert document.querySelector('header') matches the HTML element (not a class containing 'header'). div.page-header does NOT satisfy U6.

rules.rule: Page must have a <main> landmark
rules.metric: presence of <main> element
rules.threshold: <main> present (build new pages with <main class=page>)
rules.detect: Assert document.querySelector('main') exists.

rules.rule: Toast container must have ARIA so mobile screen readers (VoiceOver/TalkBack) announce it
rules.metric: role and aria-live attributes on toast container
rules.threshold: role='alert' AND aria-live='polite'
rules.detect: Assert toast container (#toast) has role='alert' and aria-live='polite'. Missing = save/error/approval confirmations are silent for VoiceOver/TalkBack users.

rules.rule: No more than ~10 simultaneous infinite CSS animations on mobile (iOS WebKit GPU-memory tab kill)
rules.metric: count of concurrently-running infinite CSS animations at mobile width
rules.threshold: < 10 concurrent infinite animations on mobile
rules.detect: At <=767px enumerate elements with getComputedStyle.animationIterationCount==='infinite' that are decorative (exclude whitelisted functional: .spinner/.skeleton/shimmer/.typing-dot/spin/blink/pulse/mic-pulse/dot-pulse/.live/#refresh-icon); assert count < 10. Particle/beam generators must be guarded by if(window.innerWidth<768)return.

rules.rule: Decorative infinite animations must have a @media(max-width:767px){animation:none} kill
rules.metric: presence of a mobile animation:none kill rule for each decorative infinite animation
rules.threshold: every decorative infinite animation has a max-width:767px animation:none rule (with !important if a later inline <style> re-declares it)
rules.detect: Static regex (mobile_decorative_anim_has_mobile_kill): trigger on animation:<decorative>...infinite; require @media(max-width:767px){...animation:none}. Also detect animation_cascade_order: a selector killed in a media query but re-declared with infinite animation in a LATER style block (needs !important).

rules.rule: will-change:filter must be overridden to will-change:auto on mobile (most expensive mobile GPU property)
rules.metric: presence of will-change:auto override at max-width:767px for any will-change:filter declaration
rules.threshold: every will-change:filter has a max-width:767px will-change:auto override
rules.detect: Extract <style> blocks only (not full file - avoids false positives on HTML text); if will-change:filter present, assert a @media(max-width:767px){will-change:auto} override exists.

rules.rule:
Pages using html2pdf must inject page-break-inside:avoid on

(and tr/td/th and div content blocks)
rules.metric: presence of #report-panel p {page-break-inside:avoid} when html2pdf() is used
rules.threshold: page-break-inside:avoid rule present on p (plus tr,td,th,h2/h3 break-after,.result-highlight,.sig-block, and classed div blocks)
rules.detect: Static (mobile_pdf_pagebreak_covers_p): if html2pdf( appears, assert /#report-panel\s+p\s*\{[^}]*page-break-inside\s*:\s*avoid/. Inline-styled divs need a class to be targetable.

rules.rule: html2pdf pagebreak config must NOT use avoid-all (causes blank first page) and pagebreak option must be present
rules.metric: html2pdf().set pagebreak mode value
rules.threshold: pagebreak: {mode:['css']} only; never includes 'avoid-all'; pagebreak option must exist
rules.detect: Search every PDF-generation function for 'avoid-all' (must be absent) and confirm a pagebreak option is present in html2pdf().set({...}) (missing pagebreak silently ignores all CSS page-break rules).

rules.rule: Results card layouts in PDF reports must be HTML <table>, not display:grid (grid page-break unreliable in html2pdf)
rules.metric: element type used for multi-card results layout in a PDF-downloadable report
rules.threshold: must be <table> with page-break-inside:avoid on it; not a display:grid container
rules.detect: In report templates that download as PDF, assert no display:grid container is used for result cards; require <table> with page-break-inside:avoid;break-inside:avoid.

rules.rule: Dynamically injected tables / fixed-px grids must be wrapped in an overflow-scroll container
rules.metric: presence of .table-wrap (overflow-x:auto) around every injected <table> or inline grid-template-columns with fixed px
rules.threshold: every dynamic <table> / fixed-px grid wrapped in overflow-x:auto container
rules.detect: Find <table> or style='grid-template-columns:...px...' produced via innerHTML; assert each is inside a .table-wrap (overflow-x:auto;-webkit-overflow-scrolling:touch) and the wrapper CSS exists in the page <style> block.

rules.rule: White report panels must have a @media(max-width:640px) padding override
rules.metric: presence of a max-width:640px padding override on #report-panel
rules.threshold: report panel padding reduced (e.g. 1rem) at <=640px
rules.detect: Assert any white report panel has a @media(max-width:640px){padding:1rem...} override; 2.5rem padding at 375px leaves only ~295px content width and overflows 3+ column tables.

rules.rule: No bottom-right page FAB occluded by the global shell stack (conn-chip / nav-hub / feedback)
rules.metric: document.elementFromPoint(FAB center) and remaining unobstructed clickable area
rules.threshold: FAB center returns the FAB itself; not occluded into a thin strip (e.g. 52x8) by shell overlays
rules.detect: At 390px, for any page .fab/#fab-post call document.elementFromPoint(centerX,centerY) and confirm it is the FAB, not #wh-conn-chip/#wh-hub. axe target-size can flag a >=44 FAB as 'partially obscured'. Resolution: move page FABs bottom-left on mobile (@media(max-width:767px){left:24px;right:auto}).

rules.rule: PWA service worker CACHE_NAME must be bumped when any SHELL_FILE changes (stale-cache check)
rules.metric: git commit timestamp of any SHELL_FILE vs sw.js (sw_cache_staleness)
rules.threshold: no SHELL_FILE committed more recently than sw.js
rules.detect: validate_pwa.py sw_cache_staleness compares git timestamps of SHELL_FILES vs sw.js; FAIL means CACHE_NAME must be bumped. (Build-time check, not in-page.)

rules.rule: No hover-only interactions - everything must work with touch (hover states do nothing on touch)
rules.basis: skill-rule (WCAG-POUR Operable)
rules.detect: During the journey, identify any function/affordance that only reveals or activates on hover (tooltips, menus, controls). Flag if there is no touch-equivalent (tap/long-press).

rules.rule: Touch feedback (:active state) on all interactive elements
rules.basis: skill-rule (Norman-feedback / Nielsen-1 visibility of system status)
rules.detect: Tap buttons/rows/icon-buttons; observe whether there is immediate visual touch feedback (opacity/scale change). Absence of any :active feedback = flag.

rules.rule: Primary actions must be thumb-reachable at the bottom of the screen (thumb zone first)
rules.basis: skill-rule (Norman-affordance / mobile ergonomics)
rules.detect: Identify each page's primary action; judge whether it sits in the bottom thumb zone vs stranded at the top/corner. Field workers (often gloved) need one-thumb reach.

rules.rule: No content under the iOS safe area (notch top, home indicator bottom)
rules.basis: skill-rule (WCAG-POUR / platform)
rules.detect: At a notched-device profile, check whether any interactive/important content is clipped under the top notch or bottom home-indicator gesture bar.

rules.rule: Modals must be scrollable if taller than the viewport
rules.basis: skill-rule (Nielsen-3 user control / WCAG-POUR)
rules.detect: Open each modal/dialog at 375px; if content exceeds viewport height, confirm it scrolls (overflow-y auto) and all actions remain reachable. Flag if content/buttons are cut off and unreachable.

rules.rule: App works offline or shows a clear offline message; data-entry forms queue offline with visible status
rules.basis: skill-rule (Nielsen-1 visibility of status / Nielsen-9 error recovery)
rules.detect: Go offline during a journey: expect a visible #offline-banner with 'N pending' badge, 'Pending sync' indicator on queued entries, and an on-reconnect 'X entries synced' toast. Absence on a data-entry page = flag.

rules.rule: Use a bottom sheet (not full-screen modal / alert dialog / new page) for field confirmation flows needing 2-3 extra fields
rules.basis: skill-rule (Norman-mapping / Nielsen-4 consistency with native patterns)
rules.detect: When an action needs extra input before completing (e.g. 'mark PM done' -> findings/action), judge whether it uses a thumb-reachable bottom sheet vs a disorienting full-screen modal, alert/confirm dialog, or a navigation to a new page.

rules.rule: Clear panel content synchronously before an await when the user changes the panel context (no stale data on slow mobile)
rules.basis: skill-rule (Nielsen-1 visibility of system status)
rules.detect: Select a new asset/tab on a slow connection; if the previous selection's data lingers visibly for seconds during the in-flight fetch (looks like a bug), flag. Panel should clear/empty immediately, then show loading.

rules.rule: Replace native <select> with an inline custom chip/tab picker when it renders >~5 browsable options
rules.basis: skill-rule (Nielsen-2 match between system & real world / Norman-affordance)
rules.detect: For each <select> populated from a JS array/DB with >~5 options (or options with secondary metadata, or other fields depending on the choice), judge whether a browsable picker (chips in a bottom sheet) would serve better than the native full-screen iOS spinner.

rules.rule: Pill/tab rows with 4+ items must wrap (flex-wrap) to a 2-per-row grid, not overflow off-screen
rules.basis: skill-rule (Nielsen-6 recognition / discoverability)
rules.detect: At 375px inspect any pill/discipline/category row with 4+ items; if the last pill is clipped off-screen with no scroll indicator the user can't discover it -> flag (should flex-wrap with min-width calc(50%-gap)).

rules.rule: Dynamically revealed panels/results must scroll into view on mobile
rules.basis: skill-rule (Nielsen-1 visibility of system status)
rules.detect: Trigger a dynamic reveal (calc result, BOM/SOW panel, logbook entry after submit, modal confirmation) at <768px; if the revealed content lands below the fold and the page does not scrollIntoView, the user thinks the action failed -> flag.

rules.rule: Tappable <div onclick> (cards, calendar slots, list rows) must be keyboard/switch/screen-reader operable
rules.basis: WCAG-POUR Operable (keyboard) / skill-rule
rules.detect: For each tappable div with onclick, confirm role='button' + tabindex='0' + Enter/Space keydown handler + a :focus-visible ring. A tappable div invisible to keyboard/switch/TalkBack = flag (L2 interaction audit passes them; only journey catches it).

rules.rule: PWA install button must always be visible (never hidden behind beforeinstallprompt) with platform-specific tap handling
rules.basis: skill-rule (Nielsen-1 visibility / Norman-discoverability)
rules.detect: Confirm an install affordance is shown to first-time visitors (not gated on beforeinstallprompt). On tap it should handle Android/Chrome (deferred prompt), iOS Safari (Share->Add to Home Screen steps), iOS Chrome (open in Safari warning), desktop Chrome (address-bar icon hint). Hide only after confirmed install.

rules.rule: iOS: never use webkitSpeechRecognition (truthy but aborts immediately); fall back to MediaRecorder + server transcription
rules.basis: skill-rule (platform behavior / Nielsen-9 error recovery)
rules.detect: On a voice/speech feature under an iOS UA, confirm code excludes iOS before using SpeechRecognition/webkitSpeechRecognition and falls through to MediaRecorder+server (Groq Whisper). A bare window.SpeechRecognition||window.webkitSpeechRecognition check on iOS = broken feature.

rules.rule: Label PDF-export buttons 'Download / View PDF' or 'Save PDF' (iOS opens a viewer, does not save)
rules.basis: skill-rule (Nielsen-2 match real world / set correct expectations)
rules.detect: Inspect PDF-export button label; if it implies a guaranteed file save (misleads iOS users where Safari opens the built-in viewer), suggest 'Download / View PDF' or 'Save PDF'.

rules.rule: Empty/result states must give the user a way forward (query-first team views show an empty prompt, not an endless spinner)
rules.basis: Nielsen-1 visibility of system status / skill query-first UX
rules.detect: On team/multi-user views, switching tabs should show an empty prompt with explicit filters + a Search button (query-first), not a 1-5s freeze or a spinner with no guidance. Flag tab-switch-loads-everything patterns.

rules.rule: Sticky bottom CTA bar must clear the nav-hub FAB zone and is mobile-only
rules.basis: skill-rule (spatial layout / Norman-affordance)
rules.detect: If a page has a full-width sticky bottom CTA bar, confirm padding-right:84px so the button text isn't hidden behind the nav-hub FAB, that it is md:hidden (desktop never sees it), and aria-hidden toggles with visibility. Two action FABs sharing the bottom-right corner = Single-FAB-Hub anti-pattern.

rules.rule: Two action FABs must not share the bottom-right corner with the global shell stack (Single-FAB-Hub anti-pattern)
rules.basis: skill-rule / Norman (avoid crowding the primary-action zone)
rules.detect: Audit bottom-right occupancy at 390px (nav-hub FAB, conn-chip, feedback FAB, ai-widget, bottom-nav). Flag if a page adds its own fixed action FAB into that already-crowded corner.

rules.rule: Buttons must be glove-operable and text glove/sunlight-legible (industrial field-worker context)
rules.basis: skill-rule (audience-specific usability / WCAG-POUR)
rules.detect: Judge whether tap targets are large enough for gloved hands and text is large/high-contrast enough for noisy, bright outdoor use; cramped controls or thin low-contrast text in primary flows = flag.

rules.rule: <summary> disclosure toggles are tap targets and must be comfortably tappable
rules.basis: skill-rule (Operable / tap-target affordance)
rules.detect: For full-width collapsible <summary> elements (engineering panels, founder section headers), confirm they read/behave as a >=44px tappable control (min-height:44px; flex; align-items:center). Default ~34-42px = flag.

rules.rule: Every interactive element (button, link, [onclick], [role=button], input[type=button|submit]) must be at least 44x44px on mobile — including buttons rendered inside JS template literals and rows that wrap a small control.
rules.metric: min(getBoundingClientRect().width, getBoundingClientRect().height) of each interactive element
rules.detect: Enumerate `button, a, [onclick], [role=button], summary, input[type=button], input[type=submit]` and read getBoundingClientRect(); flag any where min(width,height) < 44. For hidden/hive-gated els use getComputedStyle().minHeight (rect is 0 for display:none). Note: a small control inside a too-short custom row (e.g. `.oh-job-row` 36px) fails — size the ROW or the control. Battery parent-hitbox exemption only covers li/tr/.row/[role=listitem]/label.

rules.rule: Tap-target failures are frequently OCCLUSION, not the control's own size — a 56px FAB overlapping it, or the global shell over it.
rules.metric: document.elementFromPoint(cx,cy) at the control's center returns the control itself (not an overlay/FAB)
rules.threshold: elementFromPoint must equal the control or its descendant
rules.detect: For each interactive el compute center (rect.left+width/2, rect.top+height/2) and call document.elementFromPoint; if it returns a different element (e.g. `#wh-hub-fab`, page shell) the control is occluded.

rules.rule: Bottom-right nav FAB (56px, 24px from right edge = 80px occlusion zone) must not overlap a page's primary submit/save button or a full-width sticky bottom CTA bar; sticky `position:fixed;bottom:0` bars require padding-right:84px.
rules.metric: Horizontal overlap between FAB bounding box and any primary action button / sticky bottom bar at 375px
rules.threshold: 0px overlap; sticky bar container padding-right >= 84px
rules.detect: At 375px with FAB visible, get FAB rect and each `position:fixed;bottom:0` bar + submit button rect; flag if rects intersect, or if getComputedStyle(bar).paddingRight < 84px.

rules.rule: Text-entry inputs (input, textarea, select with .wh-input or any text-entry type) must be >=16px font-size to avoid iOS Safari auto-zoom on focus. Excludes checkboxes (they don't trigger zoom).
rules.metric: getComputedStyle(field).fontSize on every input/textarea/select, measured from the LIVE DOM after each dialog/draft/category renders
rules.detect: For each `input:not([type=checkbox]):not([type=radio]), textarea, select` read getComputedStyle().fontSize and flag < 16px. Must run on runtime/innerHTML-built fields too (static scan misses inline `0.8rem`/`text-sm`/`text-xs`); trigger each conditional section/category first.

rules.rule: Text must meet WCAG 2.2 AA color contrast — account for ancestor opacity compositing (a parent opacity:0.4 multiplies child text alpha down).
rules.metric: axe-core color-contrast composited fgColor vs bgColor ratio
rules.threshold: >= 4.5:1 (normal text); >= 3:1 (large text)
rules.detect: Run `axe.run(node,{runOnly:['color-contrast']})`; on a persistent fail read the real fgColor and walk getComputedStyle(ancestor).opacity up the chain. Empty/zero-data states host the faintest ghosted text (rgba(255,255,255,0.3/0.35/0.4), text-white/25) — render the empty state and re-run axe.

rules.rule: Every non-native interactive primitive (custom div/span clickable) must show a visible focus ring; native button/a/input get a UA ring for free and are exempt.
rules.metric: getComputedStyle(el).outlineStyle when focused (or presence of a :focus/:focus-visible rule for non-native)
rules.threshold: outlineStyle != 'none' (i.e. 'auto' or a real style) when focused
rules.detect: Focus the element and read getComputedStyle(el).outlineStyle; flag non-native primitives whose focused outline is 'none' and that declare no :focus rule. Do NOT flag native button/a/input.

rules.rule: A clickable's accessible name does not prove keyboard operability — a `<div onclick>` with text but no tabindex is mouse-only (WCAG 2.1.1).
rules.metric: el.tabIndex / native-interactive status for elements carrying their own onclick
rules.threshold: tabIndex >= 0 OR native interactive OR contentEditable
rules.detect: For an element with its OWN onclick attr that is not native interactive and not tabindex>=0/contentEditable, flag `click:not-keyboard-operable`.

rules.rule: Icon-only / non-text clickables must carry an accessible name (aria-label or equivalent).
rules.metric: accessible name of each button/[role=button]/clickable
rules.threshold: accessible name length > 0
rules.detect: For each interactive el with no visible text content, check for aria-label / aria-labelledby / title; flag icon-only buttons with none as Major (clickAudit accessible-name check).

rules.rule: Every form field must have a programmatic label — label[for], aria-label, aria-labelledby, or a wrapping <label>. A visible <label>X</label> with NO for and an input with NO id is NOT associated. A placeholder is never a label.
rules.metric: presence of a programmatic label association per input/select/textarea
rules.threshold: exactly one resolvable programmatic label; 0 unassociated fields
rules.detect: For each input/select/textarea check: label[for=id] exists, OR aria-label/aria-labelledby set, OR an ancestor <label> wraps it. Flag the `.field>label`+`.wh-input` no-for/no-id pattern. Placeholder presence does NOT satisfy this.

rules.rule: Inputs must use the correct input type (email/tel/number) so mobile shows the right keyboard and native validation runs; identity fields should set autocomplete.
rules.metric: type attribute vs inferred field purpose; autocomplete on name/email/phone
rules.threshold: email field type=email, phone type=tel, etc.; autocomplete present on name/email/phone
rules.detect: Infer expected type from the field key/name; flag e.g. an email field rendered as type=text. Check autocomplete attr is present on name/email/phone fields.

rules.rule: No horizontal scroll / layout overflow on mobile.
rules.metric: document.documentElement.scrollWidth - clientWidth
rules.threshold: === 0 (page); <= 2 for a fixed-width render surface like #resume-paper
rules.detect: Read documentElement.scrollWidth - clientWidth (expect 0). For a fixed-width render container assert el.scrollWidth - el.clientWidth <= 2 — stress with one long unbroken 270-char token (URL/email).

rules.rule: Any rendered report/data table with 5 or more columns must be wrapped in an overflow-x:auto container so it does not overflow the 375px viewport.
rules.metric: column count of each report <table> and overflow-x of its wrapper
rules.threshold: >=5 columns requires a wrapping element with overflow-x:auto
rules.detect: For each table with >=5 `th`/first-row cells, check the parent has getComputedStyle().overflowX === 'auto' (or 'scroll').

rules.rule: Measure mobile at a TRUE CSS viewport — read innerWidth and devicePixelRatio first, because dpr != 1 makes setViewportSize lie (390 at dpr 0.8 -> innerWidth 487).
rules.metric: window.innerWidth and window.devicePixelRatio at the intended mobile width
rules.threshold: innerWidth resolves to the intended CSS px (e.g. 390); test at 375px and 1280px
rules.detect: Read window.innerWidth + window.devicePixelRatio before measuring; to hit 390 CSS px at dpr 0.8 set the window to 390*0.8=312, then re-confirm innerWidth.

rules.rule: No garbage values rendered in value tiles (NaN cascade, undefined, [object Object], a null with a unit).
rules.metric: text content of pure-value tiles (correctness pillar T0)
rules.threshold: 0 occurrences of NaN / undefined / [object Object] / null+unit
