rules.detect: Scan value tiles for /NaN|undefined|\[object Object\]/ and a bare null adjacent to a unit; battery referee() T0 fires Major on these.

rules.rule: Percentage value tiles must be in range (pure-value tiles only, not prose like '120% of target').
rules.metric: numeric % of pure-value tiles
rules.threshold: 0% <= value <= 100% (out-of-range -> Major for <0, Minor for >100)
rules.detect: Parse pure-value `%` tiles; flag negative as Major and >100 as Minor (skip prose-containing tiles).

rules.rule: No stuck loader — a spinner/skeleton still visible after load (and after data settles) is an unresolved fetch; every report renderer must end with showReport() so the spinner clears.
rules.metric: visibility of any loading spinner/skeleton/'Loading…' placeholder after content settle
rules.threshold: 0 visible loaders post-load
rules.detect: After awaiting supabase ready + a settle tick, query spinner/skeleton/text 'Loading…' nodes and check offsetParent!==null; a still-visible loader = Major.

rules.rule: Toast / status message container must announce to screen readers.
rules.metric: role + aria-live on the toast container; role on status regions
rules.threshold: toast: role='alert' aria-live='polite'; status regions: role='status' aria-live='polite'
rules.detect: Select the toast container (e.g. #toast / #toast-msg) and assert role==='alert' && aria-live==='polite'. Status regions need role='status' aria-live='polite'.

rules.rule: Toggle / pill / selectable buttons must expose pressed state, and an .active/[aria-selected] variant must look visibly different from its base.
rules.metric: aria-pressed presence on toggles; computed-style delta between active and base variant
rules.threshold: toggles have aria-pressed; active variant's computed style != base (not identical)
rules.detect: For toggle buttons check aria-pressed attr exists. For .active/[aria-selected] variants compare key computed styles (bg/border/color) to the base class; identical = invisible-selection Major (statesAudit).

rules.rule: Every page must declare a <main> landmark, viewport-fit=cover, and a manifest link (new-page surface contract).
rules.metric: presence of <main>, meta viewport viewport-fit=cover, link rel=manifest
rules.threshold: all three present
rules.detect: document.querySelector('main') !== null; viewport meta content includes 'viewport-fit=cover'; link[rel=manifest] present. (A page built with <div class=page> fails.)

rules.rule: A count badge must equal the rows it labels and a total must equal the sum of its rows (annotated correctness, T1).
rules.metric: count badge value vs matched row count; total vs Σ of annotated column
rules.threshold: badge == count(rows); total == Σ rows (within tolerance)
rules.detect: Where `[data-count-of=sel]` / `[data-sum-of=rowSel][data-sum-col=i]` annotations exist, compare the displayed number to document.querySelectorAll(sel).length / the column sum.

rules.rule: A KPI tile that doubles as a filter must, when tapped, render exactly the count it advertised; a filter must never GROW its row count; tabs must change panel content.
rules.metric: visible row count after tapping a KPI-filter vs the KPI value; row count before/after a filter; panel content signature before/after a tab
rules.threshold: tappedKPI value == visible rows; filtered rows <= pre-filter rows; each tab changes the panel signature
rules.detect: act(tap KPI) then count `> .card`/list rows (visible only if it hides rather than re-renders); flag mismatch. A tab that does not change panel content = dead-tab Major (T2 behavior).

rules.rule: Every inline onclick handler must reference a defined function (a dead button that throws on click) and no clickable should dead-end.
rules.metric: typeof window[fn] for each inline onclick='fn()'; href value for anchors
rules.threshold: typeof === 'function'; no href='#'/''/javascript:void with no handler
rules.detect: Parse each inline onclick, assert typeof window[fnName]==='function'. Flag a[href='#'|''|javascript:void(0)] with no click handler as a dead link.

rules.rule: Supabase-using pages must have the Supabase CDN <script> in <head> (its absence silently crashes all JS); a referenced object 404/PGRST205 in console is a real defect.
rules.metric: count of supabase-js script tags; console for 404/PGRST205 on rest/rpc
rules.threshold: >=1 supabase-js script when supabase.createClient is used; 0 object-missing console errors
rules.detect: Check for the supabase-js CDN tag; monitor console/network for 404 or PGRST205 on /rest/v1/ or .rpc() calls (a referenced table/RPC missing in this environment).

rules.rule: Empty / first-time / no-data state must be handled gracefully (helpful guidance, not a blank or broken screen) — and is ROLE-gated, so it only renders for a user/hive with no data.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Help users; aesthetic minimalist); skill-rule (data state is a test dimension)
rules.detect: Sign in as a worker/hive with no rows (or force #empty-state/#no-results visible) and judge: is there a clear next action, or a blank/broken/ghosted screen? Also check empty-state text isn't an illegible faint color.

rules.rule: Loading / slow-connection state must be handled — show a skeleton/spinner that resolves, never a perpetual 'Loading…' or stuck shimmer.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Visibility of system status)
rules.detect: Throttle/observe initial load; confirm a loading affordance appears and is replaced by content, with no tile stuck on shimmer or 'Loading…'.

rules.rule: Error state must be handled — Supabase down / failed fetch / offline leaves a calm message, never a stuck spinner or uncaught rejection. AI/network buttons must be tested offline: assert BOTH the fallback result AND that the messaging is reassuring (not a raw 'rate limited'/'check connection' error).
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Error recovery, help users recognize errors); skill-rule (test the throw/timeout path)
rules.detect: Override window.fetch to reject/abort for the one endpoint (or go offline); confirm no infinite spinner, no uncaught rejection, and the toast/message reads /offline/ rather than a raw error. Cover both timeout (returns null) and network-error (throws).

rules.rule: The displayed VALUE must be correct, not just present — it must read the canonical v_*_truth source (not a raw table/local re-derivation), use the correct formula, agree across surfaces, and match the DB row, scoped correctly to the role (worker=own hive, supervisor=aggregate, solo=solo).
rules.basis: skill-rule (WHAT axis: correctness/parity/provenance); WCAG-POUR (Understandable)
rules.detect: For each KPI read the live network filter, recompute from raw v_*_truth, and compare. Same population across two surfaces -> assert equality; different granularity -> direction-only. A clean ~integer ratio between displayed and recomputed = a hidden scope filter (e.g. worker_name).

rules.rule: Same-NAMED is not same-DERIVATION — a cross-page number 'drift' (e.g. Overdue 2 vs 3) is VERIFY-FIRST, not auto-Major: read each tile's sub-text + data-rag-label + backing view; if they count different units the fix is RELABEL/differentiate, never 'fix the math'.
rules.basis: skill-rule (parity trap); Norman-N (mapping/consistency)
rules.detect: On a journey number mismatch, compare the two tiles' data-rag-label and sub-text and the backing RPC/view; only flag if they truly count the same unit. Otherwise raise a relabel/differentiate critic candidate.

rules.rule: A control landing 'correctly' has three more axes beyond no-error: WHERE (right modal/page/filter direction), WHEN (effect arrives, no stuck/reverting state), and WHO (right outcome per role — supervisor=full, worker on a supervisor-only control gets a helpful 'ask your supervisor' not a raw error, solo is guided not stranded).
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Match system & real world; user control); skill-rule (Arrange-Act-Assert role matrix)
rules.detect: Click each safe control and assert the RIGHT modal/page appeared (by id/title), the list changed in the right direction, the effect arrived within a timeout, and re-run per identity (solo/worker/supervisor) to confirm the landing is correct for that role.

rules.rule: Redundancy / IA: a job-to-be-done served by >1 page, a control reachable >=3 ways, or a KPI/logic computed in >1 place is a redundancy DEFECT — collapse to one shared source so it can't drift (drift in a re-derived KPI is what lets WHAT-bugs survive).
rules.basis: skill-rule (editor-with-a-map / function inventory); UX laws (Hick, Tesler, Jakob)
rules.detect: Build a function inventory (action/KPI/capture x which pages expose it); flag jobs on >1 page, controls reachable >=3 ways, KPIs computed in >1 place. Surface as a critic candidate for human disposition — never auto-collapse UI.

rules.rule: A 'scan X' affordance without a matching 'generate/print X' affordance is a half-loop IA defect — the user is asked to scan a code the app never lets them create.
rules.basis: skill-rule (half-features); Norman-N (closing the loop)
rules.detect: For any scan/decode feature, check the repo/page for a corresponding generate/print affordance; absence is a Major IA finding (build the generator).

rules.rule: Every modal / bottom sheet gets the full a11y battery: role='dialog', aria-modal='true', an accessible name, ESC closes it, focus is trapped (Tab from last wraps to first, Shift+Tab from first to last), and on close focus returns to the opener. Hand-rolled modals drift from the helper's bar.
rules.basis: WCAG-POUR (Operable: keyboard, focus order); Nielsen-N (user control & freedom)
rules.detect: For each modal assert role/aria-modal/accessible-name; press ESC and confirm close; Tab through to verify focus stays inside and wraps; confirm focus returns to the triggering element on close.

rules.rule: A destructive/remove action must be UNDOABLE, and an X/close that collected input must save state the same as the primary action — silent state loss on X looks like a bug.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (User control & freedom / undo); skill-rule (recovery is first-class for a novice audience)
rules.detect: Remove an item then click Undo and assert the value returns; type in a modal, dismiss via X, reopen and assert the input survived (matches the Done/Save behavior).

rules.rule: A 'what's blocking' / checklist must list only UNMET criteria — a satisfied requirement shown as a blocker (e.g. '30 of 5 templates registered') is wrong.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Match system & real world; minimalist)
rules.detect: Read the blocker/empty-state string and confirm every listed item is genuinely unmet; flag any satisfied criterion still shown.

rules.rule: A count pill ('N entries / N open / N total') must use a COUNT query, not array.length of a paged/.limit() fetch — otherwise it understates the truth and contradicts other surfaces.
rules.basis: skill-rule (count-badge over a limited window); Nielsen-N (visibility of true status)
rules.detect: For a user whose data exceeds the page cap, compare the pill to an exact count; if it equals the cap (e.g. 200) while the DB has more, it's array.length of a paged fetch.

rules.rule: A deep-link tile ('9 PM overdue — open PM Scheduler') must match the destination's granularity/count on click-through; a source/destination mismatch (items vs assets) is a contradiction the user sees.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (Consistency & standards); skill-rule (cross-surface granularity)
rules.detect: Read the source tile count and the destination page's count for the same scope; assert they agree at the same granularity (or differentiate the labels).

rules.rule: AI/extracted/auto-filled data must pass an editable accept/edit/reject checklist with provenance labels and undo before it lands — nothing applied silently. Test empty extraction, hostile/huge response, all-unchecked confirm.
rules.basis: Nielsen-N (User control; visibility); skill-rule (AI output must be curated)
rules.detect: Drive an AI-fill feature and confirm there is an accept/edit/reject step, source/provenance labels, and undo; exercise empty, oversized, and all-unchecked cases.

rules.rule: Sign-out then sign-in must restore user-owned state (hive membership, role, preferences) from the DB — the user should land where they were, not at an empty/create-or-join screen.
rules.basis: skill-rule (localStorage state recovery); Nielsen-N (consistency, error prevention)
rules.detect: Sign out, sign back in, visit every page and confirm the user is not dropped to an empty/create state and role/hive are intact.

rules.rule: Per-role landing must be coherent: a worker hitting a supervisor-only control gets a helpful denial (not the action, not a raw error, not a dead-end); a solo/no-hive user is guided to the hive gate, not stranded; a control must not land everyone in the same place regardless of permission.
rules.basis: skill-rule (role-crossed Assert); Nielsen-N (help users; error recovery)
rules.detect: Re-run each interaction as solo/worker/supervisor and judge whether the outcome is appropriate and recoverable for that identity; a denial that dead-ends or a permission-blind landing is a finding.

rules.rule: Empty-states and faint variants are where illegible 'ghosted' text lives — judge readability of muted/disabled/secondary text against real backgrounds, not just primary body copy.
rules.basis: WCAG-POUR (Perceivable); Designer/skill-rule (muted-text class is systemic)
rules.detect: Render empty/zero states and disabled/secondary text and judge legibility; flag rgba-white low-alpha or opacity-reduced containers (often a whole class, not one node).

rules.rule: Core Web Vitals (LCP/INP/CLS) judgments are informational in a live MCP walk and must NOT be filed as confirmed defects — late fetch+eval boot mis-captures CLS/LCP; real CWV needs an observer installed before navigation (a spec, not the live walk).
rules.basis: skill-rule (live-boot CWV is late-capture); Performance/WCAG-POUR
rules.detect: If reading CWV via late-injected battery, log LCP<=2.5s / INP<=200ms / CLS<=0.1 as informational only; defer the verdict to an addInitScript-based perf spec.

rules.rule: When reading 'what page/heading is this', filter by visibility (offsetParent!==null) — querySelector('h1,h2') returns hidden gate headings (#hive-gate 'needs a hive') and can fake a 'gated out' verdict.
rules.basis: skill-rule (hidden-heading false read); measurement hygiene
rules.detect: When identifying page state, only consider elements with offsetParent!==null or a non-none computed display; confirm the real wrapper (page-wrap vs hive-gate) is visible before concluding.

rules.rule: LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) must be at most 2500ms ('good' gate)
rules.metric: LCP
rules.detect: web-vitals API via buffered PerformanceObserver measured at navigation start: new PerformanceObserver(list=>...).observe({type:'largest-contentful-paint', buffered:true}); take the last LCP entry's startTime. MUST start capture at nav-start (page.addInitScript before goto, or web-vitals lib in <head>) — late-boot/mid-session injection overcounts LCP and is informational only.

rules.rule: INP (Interaction to Next Paint) must be at most 200ms ('good' gate); requires a real interaction to register
rules.metric: INP
rules.detect: web-vitals API: INP needs a real interaction — drive one click, then re-read (PerformanceObserver type 'event'/'first-input' or the web-vitals onINP callback). __UFAI.cwv() in ufai_battery.js does this. Without an interaction INP is unmeasured.

rules.rule: CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) must be at most 0.1 ('good' gate)
rules.metric: CLS
rules.detect: web-vitals API via buffered PerformanceObserver started at navigation start: observe({type:'layout-shift', buffered:true}), sum value of entries where !hadRecentInput. MUST instrument at nav-start (addInitScript/in-head web-vitals) — late-boot capture attributes post-load shifts (lazy lists, late cards) to CLS and overcounts (e.g. false 0.596). Layout-shift 'sources' are NOT retained after settle, so culprit-node listing requires nav-start instrumentation. Known driver: async data pop-in (KPI tiles + alert banner) with no reserved space (index dashboard measured 0.077-0.116).

rules.rule: Async-rendered content slots (KPI tile grid, alert banner) must reserve space (min-height / skeleton) so the page does not reflow on data arrival
rules.metric: reserved-space-for-async-slots
rules.threshold: tile grid + alert slot have non-zero min-height/skeleton placeholder before data returns (so CLS contribution from pop-in ~0)
rules.detect: Inspect DOM before async data resolves: query the KPI tile grid container and signature-alert banner slot; assert computed min-height > 0 OR a skeleton placeholder element is present. Corroborate with a CLS delta measured across the data-load window being near zero.

rules.rule: Loading states/skeletons must be shown immediately while data fetches (no blank slot before first render)
rules.metric: loading-skeleton-present-during-fetch
rules.threshold: a loading state element is in the DOM/visible at first paint while async data is pending (board panels show a skeleton immediately)
rules.detect: At page load before data resolves, check each data-driven panel for a visible loading/skeleton element (e.g. [class*='skeleton'], [aria-busy='true'], spinner). Flag panels that render empty/blank with no placeholder while a fetch is in flight.

rules.rule: Images must have explicit width and height attributes to prevent layout shift
rules.metric: img-explicit-dimensions
rules.threshold: every has both width and height attributes (or CSS aspect-ratio) set
rules.detect: DOM: [...document.querySelectorAll('img')].filter(i => !i.getAttribute('width') || !i.getAttribute('height')) — any match is a CLS-risk finding. (axe rule overlap: none direct; this is a DOM attribute check.)

rules.rule: No render-blocking CDN/script tags in <head> without defer (except Tailwind, which is a documented known risk)
rules.metric: render-blocking-head-scripts
rules.threshold: 0 synchronous <script src> in <head> lacking defer/async, EXCEPT cdn.tailwindcss.com (documented exception — needs DOM scan before first paint)
rules.detect: DOM: [...document.head.querySelectorAll('script[src]')].filter(s => !s.defer && !s.async && !s.src.includes('tailwindcss')) — any match is render-blocking. Supabase JS specifically must NOT be in <head> (must load at end of <body>).

rules.rule: Supabase JS CDN script must be loaded at end of <body>, not in <head>
rules.metric: supabase-js-load-position
rules.threshold: the @supabase/supabase-js <script src> appears in <body> (near end), not in <head>
rules.detect: DOM: check whether script[src*='@supabase/supabase-js'] is a descendant of document.head (fail) vs document.body (pass). Canonical reference: logbook.html loads it at bottom of body.

rules.rule: Floating AI widget script must be deferred / loaded last so it does not block page render
rules.metric: ai-widget-script-load-order
rules.threshold: floating-ai.js is the last <script> (loaded after supabase-js at end of body) and/or deferred — must not block initial render
rules.detect: DOM: locate script[src*='floating-ai']; assert it is among the last scripts in <body> and not in <head>. Corroborate it is not the LCP-blocking resource via resource timing.

rules.rule: On mobile viewport, count of concurrent infinite CSS animations must be under 10 (GPU memory crash budget)
rules.metric: concurrent-infinite-css-animations-mobile
rules.threshold: < 10 simultaneous infinite CSS animations when innerWidth < 768
rules.detect: Resize to mobile (e.g. 375px). Count elements with a running infinite animation: [...document.querySelectorAll('*')].filter(el => { const s=getComputedStyle(el); return s.animationName!=='none' && s.animationIterationCount==='infinite'; }).length — flag if >= 10.

rules.rule: Any element with will-change: filter must have a mobile override to will-change: auto (crash risk on mobile)
rules.metric: will-change-filter-mobile-override
rules.threshold: 0 elements with computed will-change containing 'filter' on a mobile viewport (<=767px)
rules.detect: Resize to <=767px width, then DOM: [...document.querySelectorAll('*')].filter(el => getComputedStyle(el).willChange.includes('filter')) — any match means the @media(max-width:767px){ will-change:auto } override is missing. Example offender class: .logo-glow-img.

rules.rule: Particle/beam decorative generators must be gated off on mobile (not created when innerWidth < 768)
rules.metric: particle-beam-system-gated-on-mobile
rules.threshold: 0 dynamically generated particle/beam decorative elements present at mobile width (<768px)
rules.detect: Load at mobile width (<768px) and count beam/particle decorative nodes (e.g. [class*='beam'], [class*='particle']) — should be 0 because creation is gated by `if (window.innerWidth < 768) return;`.

rules.rule: Animated elements must animate only transform/opacity (GPU-composited); transform/scale/rotate animations need will-change: transform
rules.metric: animation-composited-properties
rules.threshold: keyframe-animated properties are limited to transform and opacity (GPU-composited); non-composited animated properties (top/left/width/height/margin) are findings; transform-animated heavy elements have will-change: transform
rules.detect: Parse document.styleSheets @keyframes rules; flag any keyframe that animates layout/paint properties other than transform/opacity (and filter, which needs its own will-change:filter). Cross-check animated elements for the matching will-change value via getComputedStyle.

rules.rule: Filter animations (drop-shadow/blur/brightness/contrast) require will-change: filter on the animated element
rules.metric: filter-animation-will-change
rules.threshold: every element whose @keyframes animates a `filter` property has computed will-change containing 'filter'
rules.detect: Find @keyframes blocks containing a `filter:` property; for each element using such an animation, assert getComputedStyle(el).willChange includes 'filter'. (On mobile this flips to the will-change:auto override rule above.)

rules.rule: Body opacity:0 -> 1 page-enter animation must have an 800ms JS safety guard to avoid a permanently blank page on slow/failed CDN
rules.metric: body-opacity-guard-present
rules.threshold: if body has a page-enter animation starting at opacity:0, a setTimeout (~800ms) fallback restores opacity:1; and body opacity reaches 1 within ~800ms of load
rules.detect: Detect body { animation: page-enter } / starting opacity 0 in CSS; then assert getComputedStyle(document.body).opacity === '1' shortly after load (e.g. by 1s) even under throttled/blocked CDN. A body stuck at opacity 0 past ~800ms is a Blocker finding.

rules.rule: A prefers-reduced-motion media query must disable the body page-enter animation
rules.metric: reduced-motion-disables-body-animation
rules.threshold: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { body { animation: none } } is present
rules.detect: Emulate prefers-reduced-motion: reduce (Playwright emulateMedia), reload, then assert getComputedStyle(document.body).animationName === 'none'. Also scan stylesheets for a prefers-reduced-motion rule targeting body animation.

rules.rule: List/feed queries must not use select('*') and must enforce .limit (bounded fetch); detail single-row queries may use select('*')
rules.metric: list-query-narrow-select-and-limit
rules.threshold: every /rest/v1/ list-style request has explicit columns (select=col,col not select=*) AND a limit (default <=50, feed pages <=30/20); no unbounded list fetch
rules.detect: Capture network via page.on('request') filtered to /rest/v1/; parse the URL query: flag requests with select=* (URL-decoded select=%2A) that are list reads, and flag list reads lacking a limit= param (and not single-row id=eq./.single). Bound: list <=50, team feed page <=30, community feed fetch 50/display 20.

rules.rule: Heavy-page REST fan-out should collapse to few calls (one RPC per heavy board), not 10+ separate dashboard queries
rules.metric: rest-call-fan-out-per-page-load
rules.threshold: a single authenticated board load fires a small number of /rest/v1/ calls (index home target collapsed 14 -> 3 via one get_hive_dashboard RPC); 10+ dashboard fan-out calls is a finding
rules.detect: Live capture: park on about:blank, clear captured array, then fresh authenticated load; count requests where url includes /rest/v1/. Flag pages firing a large fan-out (e.g. >10) of separate aggregate/count queries that an RPC could collapse.

rules.rule: Realtime subscriptions must include a hive_id (or worker_name) filter, not subscribe to the whole table
rules.metric: realtime-subscription-scoped-filter
rules.threshold: every .channel(...).on('postgres_changes', {...}) config includes a filter on hive_id (or worker_name); 0 broad table-wide subscriptions
rules.detect: Inspect realtime config in client JS / network: the postgres_changes binding must carry filter: `hive_id=eq.<id>`. A subscription with no filter is a finding. Live: check WebSocket subscribe frames for an absent filter clause.

rules.rule: No realtime connection leak: per file/page, .subscribe() count must not exceed teardown (.removeChannel/.unsubscribe) count
rules.metric: realtime-subscribe-vs-teardown-balance
rules.threshold: subscribes <= teardowns per surface (leak-risk surfaces frozen at 0); comfortable channel ceiling 500, hard 1000
rules.detect: On a page, count opened channels vs torn-down channels over a navigate-away/unmount cycle (e.g. instrument supabase.getChannels().length before/after teardown, or count .subscribe vs .removeChannel calls). subscribes > teardowns = leak finding.

rules.rule: No N+1 query pattern (a loop firing one query per item instead of a single .in() batch)
rules.metric: n-plus-1-query-pattern
rules.threshold: 0 sequences of N near-identical /rest/v1/ requests differing only by a single id (use .in() instead)
rules.detect: Live capture /rest/v1/ requests; cluster by path+columns and flag bursts of >~3 requests to the same table differing only by one filter value (id=eq.X) fired close together — the N+1 signature.

rules.rule: Parallel async init / board loads must use Promise.all / Promise.allSettled, not sequential awaits or bare back-to-back calls to the same service
rules.metric: parallel-vs-sequential-init-calls
rules.threshold: independent init data loads run concurrently (single batch of near-simultaneous requests), not staggered serial round-trips
rules.detect: Live capture request start timestamps on load; if independent same-service (Supabase) loaders fire in a serial staircase (each starting only after the prior completes) rather than near-simultaneously, that is a sequential-init finding (sequential ~400ms vs parallel ~200ms).

rules.rule: Filter/search/sort and modal-open actions must not re-query the DB (read in-memory cache); no per-keystroke or per-tap round-trip
rules.metric: no-refetch-on-filter-or-modal-open
rules.threshold: 0 /rest/v1/ requests fired on filter/search keystroke or on opening an already-loaded list item's modal (cache-first; single-row fetch only as fallback)
rules.detect: Live: after initial list load, type in a search box / change a filter and open an item modal while capturing /rest/v1/ — any new list-fetch request on keystroke or on modal-open-of-cached-item is a finding (allowed: a single-row id=eq./.single fallback when item not in cache).

rules.rule: Pages must perform acceptably under mobile network conditions (assume 4G or worse, high latency 200-400ms plant-floor WiFi) — perceived load and interactions should not feel laggy for a field worker on weak signal
rules.basis: skill-rule (How to Operate: mobile network conditions; target users field workers on mobile/weak signal)
rules.detect: Throttle to 4G/slow + ~200-400ms latency in the journey; observe whether the primary task content appears and becomes interactive promptly, or whether the worker stares at blank/spinner. Judge perceived performance against the field-worker-on-weak-signal persona.

rules.rule: Prioritise user-facing impact: fix what the field worker feels first (the slowest user-perceptible bottleneck on the critical task path), not micro-optimisations
rules.basis: skill-rule (How to Operate: prioritise user-facing impact; measure before fixing)
rules.detect: During the journey, identify the single most user-perceptible slow moment on the primary task path (e.g. board taking seconds to show data, frozen tap) and judge whether it dominates the experience; rank findings by felt impact.

rules.rule: Perceived performance must be supported by optimistic UI / immediate feedback — an action should give instant visible response rather than appearing frozen during the network round-trip
rules.basis: skill-rule (improve perceived performance: loading skeletons, optimistic UI, lazy loading) + Nielsen-1 visibility of system status
rules.detect: Trigger a save/submit/tap and watch: does the UI immediately acknowledge (spinner, disabled button, optimistic insert) or does it sit unresponsive until the server replies? A multi-hundred-ms dead zone with no feedback is a finding.

rules.rule: Team/multi-user views (team feed, supervisor dashboard) must be query-first — do not auto-load an unbounded multi-user dataset on tab switch; show an empty prompt and require an explicit Search with filters + page limit
rules.basis: skill-rule (Query-First UX — Don't Auto-Load Large Team Datasets)
rules.detect: Switch to a Team/all-members view: if it immediately fetches everyone's records (large unbounded fetch, page freeze) instead of showing 'Set filters, then tap Search' and waiting for an explicit bounded query, flag it. Personal/My views may auto-load (bounded).

rules.rule: Heavy/aggregate pages should open fast via cache-first or compute-on-first-view snapshots; data freshness must be enforced (TTL or business-day boundary) and cache-miss fallback must be silent (no error/empty state)
rules.basis: skill-rule (Cache-First Pattern with TTL; Compute-on-first-view snapshots)
rules.detect: Open a heavy analytics/dashboard page twice: second open should be near-instant (hydrated from snapshot/cache, ~0 compute calls); first open of the day pays compute. Check a freshness indicator ('Updated X min ago') and that a cache miss silently falls back to live data, never an error or blank.

rules.rule: Heavy client libraries (PDF.js, mammoth.js, SheetJS, html2pdf) must be lazy-loaded on first real use, not on initial page load; client-side image compression before vision-model upload
rules.basis: skill-rule (Resume/CV Builder lazy-load lesson)
rules.detect: On initial page load, confirm large optional libs are NOT requested; trigger the feature (upload a file / export PDF) and confirm the lib loads then. Flag any heavy lib fetched eagerly at load. Check images are downscaled (~max 1400px) before upload.

rules.rule: Animations must respect a CSS-animation budget and avoid GPU overload — do not ship dozens of concurrent infinite/decorative animations that risk a mobile WebKit memory-kill (looks like a crash: 'A problem repeatedly occurred')
rules.basis: skill-rule (CSS Animation Overload — GPU Memory Crash on Mobile) + Norman-aesthetic restraint
rules.detect: On a real/throttled mobile context, observe whether the page becomes unstable, reloads, or the tab is killed under animation load; judge decorative-animation density against the ~10 concurrent budget. A reproducible mobile crash from animations is a Blocker.

rules.rule: Render-blocking CDN dependency (Tailwind/Supabase in head) is a known blank-page risk on slow connections and should be documented/mitigated; long term Tailwind should be compiled locally
rules.basis: skill-rule (Render-Blocking CDN Scripts in <head>)
rules.detect: Under a slow/blocked-CDN simulation, watch for a blank white page for 1-5s before content appears. Judge whether the page has a usable mitigation (opacity guard fires) versus a long unguarded blank screen.

rules.rule: Visibility of system status: loading must be communicated immediately (skeletons/loading states) so the user is never staring at an unexplained blank region while data fetches
rules.basis: Nielsen-1 visibility of system status + skill-rule (loading states shown immediately)
rules.detect: On each data-driven panel during load, judge whether the user sees a clear loading affordance (skeleton/spinner/'Loading…') rather than empty space or a sudden pop-in; absence of status during a perceptible fetch is a finding.

rules.rule: Layout stability: content must not visibly jump/reflow as async data arrives (KPI tiles, alert banners, lazy lists pushing content) — reserve space for late-rendering content
rules.basis: WCAG-POUR (stable/predictable) + skill-rule (async data pop-in is the CLS driver)
rules.detect: Watch the page during the first few seconds of load: judge whether tiles/banners/cards pop in and shove existing content down (a user about to tap mis-taps a moved target). Visible jank on data arrival is a finding even if the numeric CLS edge-case passes.

rules.rule: Board resilience: one failed data panel must not blank the whole board — failing loaders should degrade gracefully (Promise.allSettled, per-panel empty/error state) so the rest renders
rules.basis: skill-rule (allSettled over all; per-panel error handling) + Nielsen-9 error recovery
rules.detect: Simulate or observe one panel's data source failing (e.g. a hive missing PM tables): judge whether the rest of the board still renders normally with that panel showing its own empty/error state, versus the entire board failing/blank.

rules.rule: Editing a record must not feel laggy from redundant round-trips — opening/saving a modal should use the cached entry, not re-fetch the list/row, to avoid user-visible save lag and edit/save race conditions
rules.basis: skill-rule (Cache the entry being edited; openModal must use cache) + Nielsen-7 efficiency
rules.detect: Open an item modal and save an edit while observing responsiveness; judge whether tapping a card or saving introduces a perceptible delay consistent with a full list re-fetch, and whether rapid edit-then-save behaves consistently (no stale bleed).
